The space $\mathrm{BMO}(\mathbb{R}^n)$ of bounded mean oscillation, seminorm $\|f\|_*=\sup_Q\fint_Q|f-f_Q|$; $L^\infty\subsetneq\mathrm{BMO}$ with $\log|x|\in\mathrm{BMO}\setminus L^\infty$; the John-Nirenberg inequality $|\{x\in Q:|f-f_Q|>\lambda\}|\le C_1|Q|e^{-C_2\lambda/\|f\|_*}$ via one-step Calderón-Zygmund stopping-time recursion ($\Phi(\lambda+2^{2n})\le 2^{-n}\Phi(\lambda)$); self-improvement to $L^p$-oscillation and exponential ($e^L$) integrability; consequences — $\mathrm{BMO}$ as the $p\to\infty$ endpoint, Calderón-Zygmund operators $L^\infty\to\mathrm{BMO}$, the Fefferman-Stein sharp maximal function $M^\#$ with $\|f\|_{L^p}\approx\|M^\#f\|_{L^p}$, the Carleson-measure and commutator characterizations, and the Fefferman duality $(H^1)^*=\mathrm{BMO}$ (stated). Stein-Shakarchi harmonic. Lean status none.


### dispersive.strichartz-estimates-tt-star

- **title**: Strichartz Estimates via the TT* Method
- **unit**: `02.21.02`
- **prerequisites**: `02.21.01` (dispersive decay estimates), `02.19.05` (Riesz/Bessel potentials, Hardy-Littlewood-Sobolev), `02.10.04` (Fourier transform, Plancherel), `02.07.06` (L^p spaces, Holder/Minkowski)
- **notes**: stub - see unit `02.21.02`. Schrödinger-admissible exponent pairs 2/q + n/r = n/2 (with the wave analogue obtained by the dimensional shift n/2 -> (n-1)/2), the homogeneous Strichartz estimate ‖e^{itΔ}f‖_{L^q_t L^r_x} ≲ ‖f‖_{L^2}, and the TT* method deriving it from the pointwise dispersive decay (02.21.01) fed into the one-dimensional Hardy-Littlewood-Sobolev inequality (02.19.05). Mixed space-time Lebesgue norms and their duality; the dual/adjoint estimate by TT* duality; the retarded (inhomogeneous) Duhamel estimate via the Christ-Kiselev truncation lemma (needs strictly ordered exponents q' < q). The abstract Keel-Tao theorem with dispersive exponent β as the only datum, the delicate endpoint q=2 (kernel |t-s|^{-1} at the HLS failure threshold, dyadic-bilinear/Lorentz-atom argument), and the failure of the (2,∞,2) corner in dimension two. Restriction-extension equivalence to Stein-Tomas. Central estimate of the dispersive chapter (Tao Ch. 2 §2.3). Lean status none: Mathlib has Fourier/Plancherel, interpolation, and L^p duality but no dispersive-PDE namespace, no propagator, no mixed-norm Bochner spaces, no abstract TT* lemma, no HLS/fractional integration, no Christ-Kiselev truncation, and no Keel-Tao bilinear endpoint.


### pde.harnack-inequality-moser-krylov-safonov

- **title**: The Harnack Inequality for Elliptic Equations (Moser and Krylov-Safonov)
- **unit**: `02.17.08`
- **prerequisites**: `pde.de-giorgi-nash-moser-holder-continuity`, `pde.alexandrov-bakelman-pucci-estimate`, `pde.laplace-equation-harmonic-functions`
- **notes**: The Harnack inequality $\sup_{B_R}u\le C\inf_{B_R}u$ for non-negative weak/strong solutions, $C=C(n,\Lambda/\lambda)$ scale-invariant. Moser route (divergence form $-\operatorname{div}(A\,Du)=0$): local boundedness (sup half) by $L^p$ iteration plus the weak Harnack (inf half), where the John-Nirenberg/BMO lemma applied to $\log u$ ferries the bound across the singular exponent $p=0$ from positive to negative powers. Krylov-Safonov route (non-divergence $a^{ij}D_{ij}u=0$, no energy structure): ABP estimate run through a Calderon-Zygmund cube decomposition gives the growth/measure lemma (weak-$L^\varepsilon$ decay of super-level sets), paired with the local max principle for the Pucci extremal $\mathcal{M}^+$. Harnack implies oscillation decay $\operatorname{osc}_{B_R}\le\theta\,\operatorname{osc}_{B_{4R}}$, $\theta=(C-1)/(C+1)$, hence interior $C^{0,\alpha}$; and implies Liouville for general operators by sending $R\to\infty$ with scale-invariant $C$. Generalises harmonic Harnack (02.13.01) to bounded-measurable / non-divergence coefficients. Lean status none.


### calderon-zygmund.riesz-transforms
The Riesz transforms $R_jf = c_n\,\mathrm{p.v.}\int (x_j-y_j)|x-y|^{-n-1}f(y)\,dy$ with bounded homogeneous-degree-zero Fourier multiplier $-i\xi_j/|\xi|$; the vector $(R_1,\dots,R_n)$ as the $n$-dimensional Hilbert transform; $L^p$-boundedness ($1<p<\infty$) as a special case of the Calderon-Zygmund theory (02.19.03); the identity $\sum_j R_j^2=-I$; the second-order representation $\partial_i\partial_j=-R_iR_j\Delta$ giving the elliptic estimate $\|\partial_i\partial_j u\|_p\le C_p\|\Delta u\|_p$ (the singular-integral input to $W^{2,p}$ regularity); and the rotational-covariance characterization. Stein Ch. III. Lean status none.


### analysis.lax-milgram-weak-solutions-elliptic

- **title**: Lax-Milgram and Existence of Weak Solutions of Elliptic Boundary-Value Problems
- **unit**: `02.16.04`
- **prerequisites**: `analysis.rellich-kondrachov-poincare` (02.16.03), `02.11.08` (Hilbert space), `numerical-pde.sobolev-spaces-h-s-and-w-k-p` (24.01.01)
- **notes**: stub — see unit `02.16.04`. The Lax-Milgram theorem: a bounded coercive (not necessarily symmetric) bilinear form $B$ on a real Hilbert space induces, via Riesz representation [02.11.08] plus a closed-range or Banach fixed-point/contraction argument, a unique solution of $B[u,v]=\langle f,v\rangle$ for every $f\in H^*$, with $\|u\|\le\beta^{-1}\|f\|$. Weak formulation of the divergence-form uniformly elliptic operator $Lu=-\partial_i(a^{ij}\partial_j u)+b^i\partial_i u+cu=f$ in $H^1_0(\Omega)$; boundedness from $L^\infty$ coefficients and Garding's inequality $B[u,u]\ge\beta\|u\|_{H^1_0}^2-\gamma\|u\|_{L^2}^2$ from uniform ellipticity, giving coercivity for $c$ large (or no drift, $c\ge0$). Existence and uniqueness of weak solutions; the Fredholm alternative for the shifted operator $L+\mu$ via the compact embedding $H^1_0\hookrightarrow\hookrightarrow L^2$ of [02.16.03], discreteness of the elliptic spectrum. Stampacchia variational inequalities, Babuska-Necas inf-sup, Galerkin/Cea quasi-optimality (the FEM foundation of [24.01.03]), and interior $H^2$ regularity by difference quotients. The general-operator existence theorem of which 24.01.03 treats the symmetric Poisson/FEM special case (Evans Ch. 6). Lean status none.


### pde.quasilinear-elliptic-leray-schauder

- **title**: Quasilinear Elliptic Equations: Gradient Estimates and Existence by Leray-Schauder
- **unit**: `02.17.09`
- **prerequisites**: `pde.de-giorgi-nash-moser-holder-continuity` (02.17.07), `pde.harnack-inequality-moser-krylov-safonov` (02.17.08), `pde.dirichlet-problem-method-of-continuity` (02.17.05), `02.11.05` (compact operators)
- **notes**: stub - see unit `02.17.09`. Quasilinear divergence-form Qu = div A(x,u,Du) + B = 0 (model: minimal surface / prescribed mean curvature, p-Laplacian) with natural-growth structure conditions. The a priori chain: maximum-principle sup bound, boundary gradient bound by barriers, interior gradient bound by differentiating the equation and applying De Giorgi-Nash-Moser (02.17.07) local boundedness to w=|Du|^2, then Holder-gradient C^beta bound. Existence by the Leray-Schauder / Schaefer fixed-point theorem: freeze-and-fix compact solution operator T (linear Schauder solvability of 02.17.05 plus Arzela-Ascoli compactness C^{2,beta} -> C^{1,beta} of 02.11.05), reducing existence to the solution-uniform C^{1,beta} bound on the homotopy Q_sigma. Nonlinear lift of the linear method of continuity (02.17.05): degree replaces Neumann series, solution-uniform estimate replaces t-uniform Schauder estimate. Bombieri-De Giorgi-Miranda interior gradient estimate (oscillation only, no boundary slope), Bernstein theorem n<=7 / Simons cone n>=8, p-Laplacian C^{1,alpha} degeneracy, monotone-A uniqueness, Jenkins-Serrin boundary-curvature condition. The capstone synthesizing 02.17.04-08. GT secs 10-11, 13, 15. Lean status none: Mathlib lacks Schaefer alternative / Leray-Schauder degree, the Holder-Schauder layer, and any quasilinear divergence-structure / gradient-estimate API.


### littlewood-paley-interpolation.hardy-spaces-atomic-decomposition

- **title**: Real-Variable Hardy Spaces H^p and the Atomic Decomposition
- **unit**: `02.20.02`
- **prerequisites**: `02.20.01` (BMO, John-Nirenberg), `02.19.03` (Calderón-Zygmund operators L^p), `02.07.06` (L^p spaces), `02.10.04` (Fourier transform, Plancherel)
- **notes**: stub - see unit `02.20.02`. Real Hardy space $H^p(\mathbb{R}^n)$, $0<p\le 1$: grand/non-tangential/radial maximal-function definition $\mathcal{M}_N f$, the $(p,q)$-atom (support in $Q$, size $\|a\|_{L^q}\le|Q|^{1/q-1/p}$, vanishing moments $\int a\,x^\alpha=0$ for $|\alpha|\le\lfloor n(1/p-1)\rfloor$), and the atomic decomposition $f=\sum_j\lambda_j a_j$ with $\|f\|_{H^p}^p\approx\inf\sum_j|\lambda_j|^p$ via Calderón-Zygmund stopping-time selection on the maximal-function level sets (Coifman-Latter). Equivalence of maximal/atomic/Riesz-transform characterizations (Fefferman-Stein); $H^1\subsetneq L^1$ with $\int f=0$ necessary; $H^1$ as the substitute for $L^1$ on which Calderón-Zygmund operators are bounded ($T:H^1\to L^1$, $H^p\to H^p$); the Fefferman duality $(H^1)^*=\mathrm{BMO}$ (pairing $\int a(\varphi-\varphi_Q)$ converges by John-Nirenberg); $H^p=L^p$ for $p>1$; Coifman-Weiss spaces of homogeneous type. Stein-Shakarchi harmonic. Lean status none.


### dispersive.local-well-posedness-semilinear-strichartz

- **title**: Local Well-Posedness for Semilinear NLS/NLW via Strichartz Contraction
- **unit**: `02.21.03`
- **prerequisites**: `02.21.02` (Strichartz estimates via TT*), `02.16.01` (Sobolev inequalities, Gagliardo-Nirenberg), `02.01.05` (metric space, Banach contraction)
- **notes**: stub - see unit `02.21.03`. The semilinear Schrodinger iu_t+Du=mu|u|^(p-1)u and semilinear wave equations; the scaling-critical Sobolev exponent s_c = n/2 - 2/(p-1) and the subcritical/critical/supercritical trichotomy; Duhamel's formula as a fixed point; local well-posedness by the Banach contraction mapping principle in a Strichartz solution space (H^s subcritical via a positive time-factor T^theta, H^{s_c} critical small-data via Cazenave-Weissler); the homogeneous and retarded Strichartz estimates (02.21.02) plus the fractional-Leibniz/Moser product estimate on the Sobolev scale (02.16.01) that close the contraction; persistence of regularity, the blowup/continuation criterion, and Lipschitz continuous dependence (the full Hadamard well-posedness package). Supercritical ill-posedness sharpness; NLW parallel with the (n-1)/2 dispersive shift. Tao Ch. 3. Lean status none: Mathlib has the Banach fixed point and L^p/Sobolev API but no propagator, Duhamel map, mixed space-time Bochner norms, Strichartz estimate, or fractional-Leibniz product estimate.


### pde.direct-method-calculus-of-variations

- **title**: The Direct Method of the Calculus of Variations
- **unit**: `02.18.04`
- **prerequisites**: `02.16.03` (Rellich-Kondrachov compactness, Poincaré), `02.16.01` (Sobolev inequalities), `09.02.02` (classical Euler-Lagrange)
- **notes**: stub - see unit `02.18.04`. Existence of minimizers of the variational integral $I[u]=\int_\Omega L(Du,u,x)\,dx$ over $W^{1,q}(\Omega)$ by the direct method: coercivity ($L\ge\alpha|p|^q-\beta$) plus reflexivity gives a weakly convergent minimizing sequence, and weak lower semicontinuity identifies the weak limit as a minimizer (Tonelli's theorem). Convexity of $p\mapsto L(p,z,x)$ is the exact criterion for weak lower semicontinuity in the scalar case (necessary and sufficient via the oscillating-laminate converse). The Euler-Lagrange equation $-\operatorname{div}D_pL+D_zL=0$ read as the vanishing first variation (weak PDE satisfied by the minimizer); uniqueness under strict convexity. Vectorial case ($u:\Omega\to\mathbb{R}^m$, $m,n\ge2$): Morrey quasiconvexity replaces convexity (convex $\Rightarrow$ polyconvex $\Rightarrow$ quasiconvex $\Rightarrow$ rank-one convex, all strict), with $\det$ a non-convex null Lagrangian; Ball's polyconvexity and nonlinear elasticity; relaxation by the convex/quasiconvex envelope; Hilbert's 19th/20th problems and the scalar/vectorial regularity divide. Distinct from the classical Euler-Lagrange ODE derivation `09.02.02`. Evans Ch. 8. Lean status none.


### littlewood-paley-interpolation.littlewood-paley-square-function

- **title**: Littlewood-Paley Theory and the Square Function
- **unit**: `02.20.03`
- **prerequisites**: `02.19.03` (Calderon-Zygmund singular integral operators, L^p), `02.07.06` (L^p spaces), `02.10.04` (Fourier transform, Plancherel), `02.11.08` (Hilbert space)
- **notes**: stub - see unit `02.20.03`. Smooth dyadic frequency decomposition $f=\sum_j\Delta_j f$ via annular Littlewood-Paley projections; the square function $Sf=(\sum_j|\Delta_j f|^2)^{1/2}$ and the theorem $\|f\|_p\sim\|Sf\|_p$ for $1<p<\infty$, proved by viewing $(\Delta_j)_j$ as an $\ell^2$-valued Calderon-Zygmund operator (vector-valued [02.19.03]), lower bound by duality via the reproducing identity $\sum_j\widetilde\Delta_j\Delta_j=I$; the Khintchine/Rademacher randomization route reducing $S$ to a uniform average of Mikhlin multipliers $T_\varepsilon=\sum_j\varepsilon_j\Delta_j$; the Stein $g$-function $g(f)=(\int_0^\infty|t\partial_t P_t f|^2,\,dt/t)^{1/2}$ from the Poisson semigroup with $\|g(f)\|_p\sim\|f\|_p$; the Lusin area integral $S_\alpha$; applications to Marcinkiewicz/Hormander-Mikhlin multipliers, Besov-Triebel-Lizorkin spaces ($L^p=\dot F^0_{p,2}$), and the Carleson-measure/BMO endpoint. Stein-Shakarchi harmonic. Lean status none.


### dispersive.conservation-laws-global-well-posedness

- **title**: Conservation Laws, Global Well-Posedness, and the Energy-Critical Problem
- **unit**: `02.21.04`
- **prerequisites**: `02.21.03` (local well-posedness via Strichartz contraction), `02.21.02` (Strichartz estimates, TT*), `02.16.01` (Sobolev/Gagliardo-Nirenberg inequalities)
- **notes**: stub - see unit `02.21.04`. Conservation of mass $M[u]=\int|u|^2$, momentum $P[u]=\operatorname{Im}\int\bar u\nabla u$, and energy $E[u]=\int(\tfrac12|\nabla u|^2+\tfrac{\mu}{p+1}|u|^{p+1})$ for NLS (and the wave energy adding $\tfrac12\|\partial_t u\|_{L^2}^2$); the regularise-conserve-pass-to-limit argument upgrading the formal $\tfrac{d}{dt}E=0$ from smooth to $H^1$ data via persistence of regularity. How an a-priori conserved quantity upgrades local to global well-posedness: defocusing ($\mu=+1$) $H^1$-subcritical GWP because the energy is coercive ($\tfrac12\|\nabla u\|^2\le E$) and the local time depends only on the $H^1$ norm, so the norm-dependent local theory iterates with a uniform time-step; mass-conservation $L^2$ theory. The energy-critical power $p=(n+2)/(n-2)$ ($s_c=1$), the degeneration of the soft iteration at criticality, and the defocusing global-existence-and-scattering theorem at survey level: Bourgain's induction-on-energy (radial, $n=3,4$), CKSTT's interaction-Morawetz removal of the radial assumption (quintic $\mathbb{R}^3$), and the Kenig-Merle concentration-compactness/rigidity road map with the Aubin-Talenti ground state $W$ as threshold. Focusing vs defocusing dichotomy; the virial identity and Glassey ($E<0$, finite variance $\Rightarrow$ blowup). Tao Ch. 3-5. Lean status none.


### pde.h2-hk-regularity-weak-elliptic-solutions

- **title**: Interior and Boundary H^2/H^k Regularity of Weak Elliptic Solutions
- **unit**: `02.17.01`
- **prerequisites**: `02.16.04` (Lax-Milgram, weak elliptic existence), `02.16.01` (Sobolev inequalities, Gagliardo-Nirenberg-Morrey), `24.01.01` (Sobolev spaces H^s, W^{k,p})
- **notes**: stub - see unit `02.17.01`. If u in H^1 is a weak solution of Lu=f (divergence form, f in L^2, C^1 leading coefficients) then u in H^2_loc with the elliptic estimate ||u||_{H^2} <= C(||f||_{L^2} + ||u||_{L^2}), proved by the Nirenberg difference-quotient method; the bootstrap f in H^k => u in H^{k+2}; up-to-the-boundary regularity on C^2 domains via tangential difference quotients plus recovery of the normal second derivative from the equation; the C^infty corollary via Sobolev embedding. The L^2/energy-method (Sobolev-scale) regularity theory, complementing the Hölder/Schauder scale (02.17.04) and the L^p Calderon-Zygmund scale (02.17.06). Evans Ch. 6.3. Lean status none.


### pde.lp-calderon-zygmund-w2p-estimates
- **title**: Lp (Calderón-Zygmund) W^{2,p} Estimates for Elliptic Equations
- **unit**: `02.17.06`
- **prerequisites**: `calderon-zygmund.riesz-transforms` (02.19.04), `pde.schauder-c2alpha-estimates` (02.17.04), `measure-theory.lp-spaces-holder-minkowski-completeness` (02.07.06)
- **notes**: stub - see unit `02.17.06`. The L^p (Calderón-Zygmund) theory for second-order elliptic equations: the interior/global W^{2,p} estimate ||u||_{W^{2,p}} <= C(||Lu||_{L^p} + ||u||_{L^p}) for 1<p<infty, built from the singular-integral bound ||d_i d_j u||_p <= C_p ||Delta u||_p (the Riesz representation d_i d_j = -R_i R_j Delta from 02.19.04) by frozen-coefficient perturbation that needs only continuity of the leading coefficients; existence of strong solutions in W^{2,p} cap W^{1,p}_0 via the method of continuity; the borderline p=infty failure (Hessian only in BMO) that forces the Schauder C^{2,alpha} theory there. Closes the L^p-data regularity forward-reference of 02.13.02; the Lebesgue-scale dual of Schauder (02.17.04). Lean status none: Mathlib has no Riesz transforms, no Calderón-Zygmund inequality, and no elliptic W^{2,p} estimate.


### analysis.fredholm-alternative-elliptic-eigenvalues

- **title**: The Fredholm Alternative and Eigenvalues for Second-Order Elliptic Operators
- **unit**: `02.16.05`
- **prerequisites**: `analysis.lax-milgram-weak-solutions-elliptic` (02.16.04), `functional-analysis.compact-operators` (02.11.05), `analysis.rellich-kondrachov-poincare` (02.16.03)
- **notes**: stub — see unit `02.16.05`. The Fredholm alternative for $Lu-\lambda u=f$ via the compact solution operator $G=(L+\mu)^{-1}$ on $L^2$ (Lax-Milgram inverse [02.16.04] composed with the Rellich compact embedding $H^1_0\hookrightarrow\hookrightarrow L^2$ [02.16.03]), Riesz-Schauder theory [02.11.05] through the dictionary $\kappa=(\lambda+\mu)^{-1}$: either unique solvability for all $f$, or finite-dimensional equal-dimension kernels of $L,L^*$ with solvability iff $f\perp\ker(L^*-\lambda)$. For symmetric $L$, a discrete real spectrum $0<\lambda_1<\lambda_2\le\cdots\to\infty$ with an $L^2$-orthonormal eigenbasis (compact-self-adjoint spectral theorem). Variational Rayleigh-quotient / Courant-Fischer min-max characterization $\lambda_k=\min_{\dim S=k}\max_{u\in S}R[u]$; domain monotonicity; simplicity and strict positivity of the principal eigenvalue $\lambda_1$ (Krein-Rutman / strong maximum principle). Weyl asymptotics, nodal-domain theorem, heat-semigroup eigenexpansion (Evans Ch. 6). Lean status none.


### pde.viscosity-solutions-hamilton-jacobi

- **title**: Viscosity Solutions of Hamilton-Jacobi Equations
- **unit**: `02.18.05`
- **prerequisites**: `02.18.04` (direct method, calculus of variations), `02.13.03` (heat equation, heat kernel, Duhamel), `09.05.02` (classical Hamilton-Jacobi equation)
- **notes**: stub - see unit `02.18.05`. The Crandall-Lions viscosity-solution theory for the evolutionary Hamilton-Jacobi equation $u_t+H(Du,x)=0$. Viscosity sub/supersolutions defined by $C^1$ test functions touching a merely continuous $u$ from above/below (equivalently the super/subdifferentials $D^+u$, $D^-u$): subsolution touched from above with $\varphi_t+H(D\varphi,x)\le0$, supersolution from below with $\ge0$. Consistency with classical solutions (the equation holds pointwise wherever $u$ is differentiable). The comparison principle by doubling of variables (penalize with $|x-y|^2/2\varepsilon$, push the maximizers together at rate $|x_\varepsilon-y_\varepsilon|^2/\varepsilon\to0$), giving uniqueness. The Hopf-Lax formula $u(x,t)=\min_y\{tL((x-y)/t)+g(y)\}$ with $L=H^*$ the Legendre transform for convex coercive $H$, and the dynamic-programming (Bellman) semigroup. Existence by vanishing viscosity ($u^\varepsilon_t+H(Du^\varepsilon)=\varepsilon\Delta u^\varepsilon$, $\varepsilon\downarrow0$) and by Perron's method (stated). Stability under uniform limits. Optimal-control interpretation: the value function is the unique viscosity solution of the Hamilton-Jacobi-Bellman equation $H(p,x)=\sup_a\{-f\cdot p-\ell\}$. Distinct from the classical Hamilton-Jacobi equation of mechanics `09.05.02` (continuation past the caustic). Evans Ch. 10. Lean status none.


### littlewood-paley-interpolation.fourier-multipliers-hormander-mikhlin

- **title**: Fourier Multipliers and the Hörmander-Mikhlin Theorem
- **unit**: `02.20.04`
- **prerequisites**: `02.20.03` (Littlewood-Paley square function), `02.19.03` (Calderon-Zygmund operators, L^p), `02.10.04` (Fourier transform, Plancherel)
- **notes**: stub - see unit `02.20.04`. Multiplier operators $T_m f=(m\widehat f)^\vee$; the $L^2$ characterization $\\T_m\\_{L^2\to L^2}=\\m\\_{L^\infty}$; the Mikhlin condition $|\partial^\alpha m|\le A|\xi|^{-|\alpha|}$ for $|\alpha|\le\lfloor n/2\rfloor+1$ (and Hormander's localized $L^2$-Sobolev form) $\Rightarrow$ $T_m$ bounded on $L^p$, $1<p<\infty$, proved by dyadic decomposition of the symbol (Littlewood-Paley [02.20.03]) into single-scale bumps whose $H^s$ ($s>n/2$) regularity becomes Hormander kernel regularity, then the Calderon-Zygmund master theorem [02.19.03]. Applications: Riesz transforms, imaginary powers $(-\Delta)^{is}$ with polynomial-in-$s$ norm growth (Stein interpolation), the Hilbert transform, the Marcinkiewicz bounded-dyadic-variation refinement on $\mathbb{R}$, and the Bochner-Riesz means $(1-|\xi|^2)_+^\delta$ (easy case $\delta>(n-1)/2$ by $L^1$ kernel; the conjecture and its restriction-theory equivalence). Stein-Shakarchi harmonic. Lean status none.


### dispersive.bourgain-xsb-spaces-low-regularity

- **title**: Bourgain X^{s,b} Spaces and Low-Regularity Well-Posedness
- **unit**: `02.21.05`
- **prerequisites**: `02.21.02` (Strichartz estimates via TT*), `02.10.04` (Fourier transform, Plancherel), `02.20.03` (Littlewood-Paley square function)
- **notes**: stub - see unit `02.21.05`. The Bourgain (dispersive Sobolev) X^{s,b} norm ‖⟨ξ⟩^s⟨τ−h(ξ)⟩^b û‖_{L^2_{τ,ξ}} adapted to a dispersion relation τ=h(ξ); the modulated-restriction identity ‖u‖_{X^{s,b}}=‖U(−t)u‖_{H^b_t H^s_x} that flattens the dispersion surface; the transference/energy embedding X^{s,b}↪C_t H^s for b>1/2 (sharp) and the transference of every Strichartz estimate (02.21.02) into the X^{0,b} scale; the time-localization lemma ‖η(t/T)u‖_{X^{s,b'}}≲T^{b−b'}‖u‖_{X^{s,b}}; the Duhamel one-modulation-power gain X^{s,b−1}→X^{s,b}; how a single bilinear/multilinear X^{s,b} estimate (Kenig-Ponce-Vega KdV bound, sharp at s>−3/4, via the cubic resonance factorisation 3ξξ₁(ξ−ξ₁); Bourgain periodic L^4 via lattice-point/divisor counting) drives local well-posedness BELOW the Strichartz-contraction threshold and even below L^2; the I-method/almost-conservation route (CKSTT) to global theory. Frequency- and modulation-dyadic Littlewood-Paley decomposition (02.20.03) organises the bilinear estimate. Tao §2.6, §4.1. Lean status none: Mathlib has no space-time Fourier transform, dispersion weight, X^{s,b} norm, propagator, or resonant-set bilinear machinery.


### analysis.galerkin-existence-parabolic

- **title**: Galerkin Existence and Energy Estimates for Second-Order Parabolic Equations
- **unit**: `02.18.01`
- **prerequisites**: `02.16.04` (Lax-Milgram, elliptic weak theory, Garding coercivity), `24.01.01` (Sobolev spaces, Bochner integral), `02.13.03` (heat equation, heat kernel, Duhamel)
- **notes**: stub - see unit `02.18.01`. Existence and uniqueness of weak solutions of the second-order parabolic problem $u_t+L(t)u=f$, $u(0)=g$ via the Faedo-Galerkin method and energy estimates. Bochner spaces $L^2(0,T;V)$ with $u'\in L^2(0,T;V^*)$, the Gelfand triple $V=H^1_0\subset H=L^2\subset V^*=H^{-1}$, the solution space $W(0,T)$ and the Lions-Aubin embedding $W(0,T)\hookrightarrow C([0,T];H)$ recovering the initial trace. Galerkin approximation by projection onto Dirichlet eigenspaces (finite-dim ODE system, Caratheodory solvability); a-priori energy estimate from Garding coercivity (02.16.04) closed by Gronwall, uniform in the truncation level; weak/weak-* compactness (Banach-Alaoglu) passage to the limit; energy-equality uniqueness; the pairing identity $\frac{d}{dt}\tfrac12\|u\|_H^2=\langle u',u\rangle$. Improved/parabolic regularity (test with $u'$), the autonomous semigroup representation $e^{-tL}$ (Hille-Yosida, Duhamel lift of 02.13.03), Aubin-Lions strong compactness for nonlinear passage, Lions' variational existence for general time-dependent forms, and the hyperbolic (conservation vs dissipation) parallel. Evans Ch. 7.1. Lean status none: Mathlib has Bochner integration, Gronwall, the spectral theorem and Banach-Alaoglu but no Gelfand triple, $W(0,T)$, Lions-Aubin embedding, or abstract parabolic Galerkin existence.


### pde.scalar-conservation-laws-entropy-solutions

- **title**: Scalar Conservation Laws: Shocks, Rankine-Hugoniot, and Entropy Solutions
- **unit**: `02.18.06`
- **prerequisites**: `02.18.05` (viscosity solutions of Hamilton-Jacobi), `02.13.04` (wave equation, finite speed), `24.01.01` (Sobolev/BV function spaces)
- **notes**: stub - see unit `02.18.06`. Weak solutions of $u_t+\operatorname{div}F(u)=0$ via the distributional pairing; characteristics $x=x_0+tF'(u_0)$ and finite-time gradient blow-up; the Rankine-Hugoniot jump $s'[u]=[F(u)]$; non-uniqueness of weak solutions and the Lax entropy condition $F'(u_\ell)\ge s\ge F'(u_r)$ / Oleinik one-sided inequality; the Lax-Oleinik formula $u=G((x-y)/t)$ as the spatial derivative of the Hopf-Lax value function (convex flux); the Riemann problem (shock vs rarefaction); Kruzhkov entropy pairs $|u-k|$ and the doubling-of-variables $L^1$-contraction uniqueness; vanishing viscosity $u^\varepsilon_t+F(u^\varepsilon)_x=\varepsilon u^\varepsilon_{xx}$. Differentiated twin of the viscosity-HJ unit 02.18.05, sharing the doubling-of-variables and Legendre-duality backbone. Evans Ch. 3.4 + 11. Lean status none: Mathlib has BV and Legendre-Fenchel but no weak/entropy-solution predicate, Rankine-Hugoniot, Kruzhkov pairs, or doubling-of-variables contraction.


### dispersive.virial-blowup-soliton-stability

- **title**: Virial Identities, Blowup, and the Soliton-Stability Outlook
- **unit**: `02.21.06`
- **prerequisites**: `02.21.04` (conservation laws, global well-posedness, energy-critical problem), `02.21.03` (local well-posedness via Strichartz contraction), `02.16.01` (Sobolev/Gagliardo-Nirenberg inequalities)
- **notes**: stub - see unit `02.21.06`. The book's closing chapter. Virial/Morawetz-type identity $\tfrac{d^2}{dt^2}\int|x|^2|u|^2 = 8\int|\nabla u|^2 + \tfrac{4n\mu(p-1)}{p+1}\int|u|^{p+1}$, reducing at focusing $\mu=-1$, $p\ge 1+4/n$ to $V''\le 16E[u_0]$; Glassey's theorem (negative-energy finite-variance focusing $H^1$ data blow up in finite time by the downward-parabola/non-negativity contradiction). The pseudoconformal symmetry at the $L^2$-critical power $p=1+4/n$ and the explicit minimal-mass blowup solution concentrating as $t\to 0$. The ground-state soliton $e^{i\omega t}\phi_\omega$ with profile $-\Delta Q+Q=Q^p$; the sharp Gagliardo-Nirenberg constant $C_{GN}=(p+1)/(2\|Q\|_{L^2}^{4/n})$ fixing the mass threshold $\|u_0\|_{L^2}<\|Q\|_{L^2}\Rightarrow$ global existence vs blowup. Orbital stability (Weinstein modulational method / Grillakis-Shatah-Strauss action-convexity criterion $d''(\omega)\gtrless 0$, equivalently $p\lessgtr 1+4/n$) vs the Berestycki-Cazenave instability dichotomy; Kwong uniqueness/non-degeneracy of $Q$. Survey/closing-outlook level. Tao Ch. 3.5-3.6. Lean status none: Mathlib lacks the propagator, Duhamel operator, weighted-variance propagation, the ground-state existence/uniqueness/non-degeneracy theory, and the GSS spectral criterion.


### analysis.galerkin-existence-hyperbolic

- **title**: Galerkin Existence and Finite Propagation Speed for Second-Order Hyperbolic Equations
- **unit**: `02.18.02`
- **prerequisites**: `02.18.01` (Galerkin existence, energy estimates, parabolic), `02.13.04` (wave equation, d'Alembert, finite propagation speed), `02.16.04` (Lax-Milgram, elliptic weak theory, Garding coercivity)
- **notes**: stub - see unit `02.18.02`. Existence and uniqueness of weak solutions of the second-order hyperbolic problem $u_{tt}+L(t)u=f$, $u(0)=g$, $u_t(0)=h$, with $L(t)$ symmetric uniformly elliptic, via the Faedo-Galerkin method and the conserved (not dissipated) energy. Solution space $u\in L^\infty(0,T;V)$, $u'\in L^\infty(0,T;H)$, $u''\in L^2(0,T;V^*)$ over the Gelfand triple $V=H^1_0\subset H=L^2\subset V^*=H^{-1}$ of 02.18.01; two initial conditions (position in $V$, velocity in $H$). Galerkin projection onto Dirichlet eigenspaces (second-order ODE system, Caratheodory solvability); the hyperbolic energy estimate from testing with the velocity $u_m'$, the kinetic-plus-form energy $E_m=\tfrac12\|u_m'\|_H^2+\tfrac12 B[u_m,u_m]$ closed by Gronwall through $\partial_t a^{ij}$, uniform in the truncation level; weak-* compactness passage to the limit; energy-method uniqueness. Conservation in place of parabolic dissipation: no smoothing, regularity of the solution equals regularity of the data propagated. The finite-propagation-speed / domain-of-dependence theorem via a local-energy flux argument on a backward characteristic cone of slope $\sqrt{\Lambda_0}$ (supremal characteristic speed), generalising the constant-coefficient backward-light-cone of 02.13.04. Advanced: first-order reduction to the unitary propagator group (Stone, 02.18.03), symmetric-hyperbolic embedding (Friedrichs 1954), improved regularity by time-differencing, autonomous eigenfunction expansion. Evans Ch. 7.2. Lean status none: Mathlib has Bochner integration, Gronwall, the spectral theorem and Banach-Alaoglu but no second-order Gelfand-triple solution space, hyperbolic Galerkin existence, local-energy functional, or finite-propagation-speed/cone-of-dependence statement.


### pde.cauchy-kovalevskaya-holmgren-uniqueness

- **title**: The Cauchy-Kovalevskaya Theorem and Holmgren Uniqueness
- **unit**: `02.13.06`
- **prerequisites**: `02.05.04` (analytic inverse/implicit function theorem, method of majorants), `02.13.04` (wave equation, characteristic surfaces, Cauchy data count), `02.14.04` (distributions, Schwartz kernel theorem, integration-by-parts duality)
- **notes**: stub - see unit `02.13.06`. The only general local existence-and-uniqueness theorem for the analytic Cauchy problem. **Cauchy-Kovalevskaya** (Cauchy 1842 calcul des limites; Kovalevskaya 1875 *Crelle* 80): an analytic Cauchy problem with analytic data on a non-characteristic analytic surface has a unique local analytic solution, proved by the **method of majorants** (reduction to a first-order quasilinear system in normal form, the forced formal power series of Proposition 1, geometric-series majorant $M(1-(z_1+\cdots+z_m)/r)^{-1}$, monotone propagation of coefficientwise domination $u\ll U$, closed-form solution of the geometric majorant problem via the analytic implicit function theorem 02.05.04). The **non-characteristic** condition $p_k(x,\nu)\neq 0$ (principal symbol non-vanishing on the conormal) is what lets the top normal derivative be solved for; characteristic surfaces (heat $\{t=0\}$, wave light cones) are excluded. **Holmgren's uniqueness theorem** (Holmgren 1901): for a linear operator with analytic coefficients on a non-characteristic surface, the $C^k$ Cauchy problem has at most one solution — even non-analytic — proved by duality (Green's identity pairing $u$ against analytic solutions $\varphi$ of the adjoint Cauchy problem $P^*\varphi=h$, Holmgren's convexifying transformation, Weierstrass density of analytic $h$). Cauchy-Kovalevskaya and Holmgren as the existence-plus-uniqueness backbone of local solvability, dual via the adjoint. Sharpness: **Lewy's 1957** smooth operator $-\partial_{x_1}-i\partial_{x_2}+2i(x_1+ix_2)\partial_{x_3}$ with no solution for generic smooth $f$, opening the Hörmander-Nirenberg-Trèves condition $(\Psi)$ local-solvability program; Métivier's analyticity-necessity. The majorant technique appears in the corpus otherwise only inside 02.05.04. Evans §4.6; John §3.3-3.5; Hörmander I §9.4. Lean status `none`: Mathlib has formal power series and `AnalyticAt` but no majorant calculus, no non-characteristic/normal-form apparatus, no Cauchy-Kovalevskaya, and no Holmgren adjoint-duality uniqueness.


### number-theory.perron-formula-mellin-inversion

- **title**: Perron's Formula and Mellin Inversion
- **unit**: `21.11.04`
- **prerequisites**: `complex-analysis.residue-theorem`, `complex-analysis.riemann-zeta`, `number-theory.riemann-zeta`
- **notes**: Perron's formula expresses the summatory function $\sum'_{n \le x} a_n$ of a Dirichlet series $A(s) = \sum a_n n^{-s}$ as the vertical contour integral $\frac{1}{2\pi i}\int_{c-i\infty}^{c+i\infty} A(s)\, x^s/s\, ds$; the truncated form replaces the infinite contour by a segment of length $2T$ with explicit error $O(\sum |a_n|(x/n)^c \min(1, 1/(T|\ln(x/n)|)))$ and is the effective version used in the contour proof of the prime number theorem. Rests on the discontinuous-integral lemma ($\frac{1}{2\pi i}\int y^s/s\, ds = 1, \tfrac12, 0$ for $y \gtrless 1$) and on Mellin inversion (the summatory function is the inverse Mellin transform of $A(s)/s$). Lean status `none` — Mellin inversion and Perron's formula are absent from Mathlib. Source: Davenport \S11, Montgomery-Vaughan \S5, Perron 1908.


### number-theory.arithmetic-functions-dirichlet-convolution-mobius

- **title**: Arithmetic Functions, Dirichlet Convolution, and Möbius Inversion
- **unit**: `21.11.01`
- **prerequisites**: `21.01.01` (divisibility, gcd, Bézout), `21.01.02` (primes, fundamental theorem of arithmetic), `21.01.04` (Fermat-Euler-Wilson, the totient)
- **notes**: stub - see unit `21.11.01`. The foundational arithmetic-function ring of analytic number theory (Apostol Ch. 2). Arithmetic functions $f:\mathbb{Z}_{>0}\to\mathbb{C}$ under pointwise addition and the **Dirichlet convolution** $(f*g)(n)=\sum_{d\mid n}f(d)g(n/d)$ form a commutative ring $(\mathcal{A},+,*)$ with identity $\varepsilon$ ($\varepsilon(1)=1$, else $0$) and units exactly $\{f:f(1)\neq 0\}$. The distinguished functions: Möbius $\mu$, Euler totient $\varphi$, divisor functions $d=\sigma_0,\sigma=\sigma_1,\sigma_k=N^k*\mathbf{1}$, von Mangoldt $\Lambda$, Liouville $\lambda$, unit $\mathbf{1}$, powers $N^k$. **Möbius inversion**: $\mu*\mathbf{1}=\varepsilon$ (the divisor-sum $\sum_{d\mid n}\mu(d)$ vanishes for $n>1$ by the binomial theorem $(1-1)^r=0$), so $g=f*\mathbf{1}\iff f=g*\mu$. Applications: Gauss $N=\varphi*\mathbf{1}\Rightarrow\varphi=\mu*N=n\prod_{p\mid n}(1-1/p)$; von Mangoldt $\log=\Lambda*\mathbf{1}\Rightarrow\Lambda=\mu*\log$; Liouville $\lambda*\mathbf{1}=\mathbb{1}_{\square}$. Multiplicativity is preserved under convolution (unique coprime factorisation of divisors); completely multiplicative $f$ has inverse $f^{-1}=\mu f$. Dirichlet inverse existence by recursion when $f(1)\neq 0$. **Dirichlet series dictionary**: $D(f)=\sum f(n)n^{-s}$ is a ring homomorphism $D(f*g)=D(f)D(g)$ with $D(\mathbf{1})=\zeta$, $D(\mu)=1/\zeta$, $D(d)=\zeta^2$, $D(\sigma_k)=\zeta(s)\zeta(s-k)$, $D(\varphi)=\zeta(s-1)/\zeta(s)$, $D(\Lambda)=-\zeta'/\zeta$; for multiplicative $f$ it factors as the Euler product $\prod_p\sum_{m\geq 0}f(p^m)p^{-ms}$ (Bell series), collapsing to $\prod_p(1-f(p)p^{-s})^{-1}$ for completely multiplicative $f$. Master: Cashwell-Everett 1959 ($\mathcal{A}$ over a field is a UFD $\cong\mathbb{C}[[x_1,x_2,\ldots]]$), Bell-series localisation, hyperbola-method averages $\sum_{n\leq x}d(n)=x\ln x+(2\gamma-1)x+O(\sqrt x)$, the Mertens function $M(x)=\sum\mu(n)$ with $M(x)=o(x)\Leftrightarrow$ PNT and $M(x)=O(x^{1/2+\epsilon})\Leftrightarrow$ RH. Originators Möbius 1832, Dirichlet 1849, Liouville 1857. Lean status none: Mathlib has the convolution ring, `moebius`, `vonMangoldt`, and abstract inversion lemmas, but no single module proving the Dirichlet-inverse recursion, the multiplicative-units subgroup, the Bell-series criterion, and the formal-Dirichlet-series Euler factorisation end to end.


### number-theory.poisson-voronoi-summation

- **title**: Poisson and Voronoi Summation
- **unit**: `21.15.01`
- **prerequisites**: `harmonic-analysis.fourier-transform-plancherel` (02.10.04), `number-theory.riemann-zeta` (21.03.01)
- **notes**: stub - see unit `21.15.01`. The Poisson summation formula $\sum_n f(n) = \sum_m \hat f(m)$ for Schwartz $f$ (proof by periodization + Fourier series); shifted/weighted/lattice forms with the covolume factor and dual lattice $\Lambda^*$; the theta transformation $\Theta(1/t)=t^{1/2}\Theta(t)$ and the zeta functional equation as its Mellin dual; the Voronoi summation formula for the divisor function $d(n)$ with the order-zero Bessel kernel $-2\pi Y_0 + 4K_0$ (functional-equation dual of $\zeta(s)^2$), and the modular-form generalization via the additively-twisted $L$-function; lattice-point / Gauss circle / Dirichlet divisor problems. Iwaniec-Kowalski Ch. 4. Lean status none.

### number-theory.gauss-jacobi-kloosterman-salie-weil-bound

- **title**: Gauss, Jacobi, Kloosterman, and Salié Sums; the Weil Bound
- **unit**: `21.15.04`
- **notes**: stub - see unit `21.15.04`. Complete exponential sums mod $p$: the general Gauss sum $g(\chi)=\sum_x \chi(x)\psi(x)$ with $|g(\chi)|=\sqrt q$ for non-trivial $\chi$; Jacobi sums $J(\chi,\lambda)$ and the Gauss-Jacobi relation $g(\chi)g(\lambda)=J(\chi,\lambda)g(\chi\lambda)$, giving point counts on diagonal curves; Kloosterman sums $S(m,n;c)=\sum e((mx+n\bar x)/c)$, twisted multiplicativity over coprime moduli; the Weil bound $|S(m,n;p)|\le 2\sqrt p$ as the Riemann hypothesis for the Artin-Schreier curve $y^p-y=mx+n/x$, with the Stepanov-Bombieri elementary route; Salié sums (quadratic twist, evaluable in closed form). Iwaniec-Kowalski Ch. 11-12. Lean status none.


### number-theory.polya-vinogradov-inequality

- **title**: The Polya-Vinogradov Inequality
- **unit**: `21.13.04`
- **prerequisites**: `21.03.02` (Dirichlet $L$-functions and characters: the character $\chi \bmod q$, Gauss sums, orthogonality), `21.01.06` (quadratic residues and the Legendre symbol: the running real-character example)
- **notes**: stub - see unit `21.13.04`. For a non-principal Dirichlet character $\chi$ modulo $q$, the incomplete character sum satisfies $|\sum_{M < n \leq M+N} \chi(n)| \leq \sqrt{q}\,\log q$ uniformly in $M, N$ (Polya 1918, Vinogradov 1918). Proof by **completion**: the finite Fourier (Gauss-sum) expansion $\chi(n) = \tau(\overline\chi)^{-1}\sum_{a \bmod q}\overline\chi(a)\,e(an/q)$ for primitive $\chi$ converts the multiplicative interval sum into a weighted sum of linear exponential sums; the geometric-series bound $|\sum_{M<n\le M+N} e(an/q)| \leq \tfrac{1}{2}\|a/q\|^{-1}$ and $|\tau(\chi)| = \sqrt q$ give $\sqrt q$ times a harmonic tail $\sum_{b\le q/2} 1/b \leq \log q$. Consequences: least quadratic non-residue $O(\sqrt q \log q)$; short-character-sum cancellation $S_\chi(M,N) = o(N)$ for $N \gg \sqrt q \log q$. Sharpness: Paley 1932 $\Omega$-result $\gg \sqrt q \log\log q$; Montgomery-Vaughan 1977 GRH-conditional $\ll \sqrt q \log\log q$; Burgess 1957/1962 short-range improvement nontrivial for $N \geq q^{1/4+\epsilon}$, giving least non-residue $\ll q^{1/(4\sqrt e)+\epsilon}$; Granville-Soundararajan 2007 pretentious structure of extremal characters. Lean status none: Mathlib has Dirichlet characters and the Gauss-sum modulus but not the primitive-character Fourier expansion, the distance-to-nearest-integer geometric bound, or the assembled Polya-Vinogradov theorem and corollaries.

### number-theory.approximate-functional-equation-convexity

- **title**: The Approximate Functional Equation, Analytic Conductor, and Convexity Bound
- **unit**: `21.13.05`
- **prerequisites**: `number-theory.riemann-zeta` (21.03.01); `complex-analysis.riemann-zeta` (06.01.16); `complex-analysis.max-modulus-schwarz` (06.01.12)
- **notes**: The approximate functional equation $L(s) = \sum_{n} a_n n^{-s} V_s(n/X) + \varepsilon\,(Q^{1-2s}\gamma(1-s)/\gamma(s)) \sum_n \overline{a_n} n^{-(1-s)} W_s(\cdot)$ obtained by shifting the contour integral of $\Lambda(s+u)X^u G(u)/u$ past the pole at $u=0$ and applying the functional equation, with optimal balance $X \asymp \sqrt{\mathfrak{q}}$ truncating both sums at length $\sqrt{\mathfrak{q}}$ up to logs. The general functional-equation framework $\Lambda(s) = Q^s\gamma(s)L(s) = \varepsilon\overline{\Lambda}(1-s)$ with gamma factors $\gamma(s)=\prod_j \Gamma_{\mathbb{R}}(s+\kappa_j)$ and root number $\varepsilon$, $|\varepsilon|=1$. The analytic conductor $\mathfrak{q}(s) = Q^2 \prod_j (|s+\kappa_j|+3)$ unifying level, height, and archimedean parameters; $\mathfrak{q} \asymp |t|$ for $\zeta$ on the critical line. The convexity (Phragmen-Lindelof) bound $L(1/2+it) \ll \mathfrak{q}^{1/4+\varepsilon}$ from the maximum-modulus principle on an unbounded strip, via the convex Lindelof function $\mu(\sigma) \leq (1-\sigma)/2$ interpolating $\mu(0)=1/2$ and $\mu(1)=0$. Subconvexity problem ($\mathfrak{q}^{1/4-\delta}$, e.g. Weyl $1/6$, Burgess $3/16$, Bourgain $13/84$) and the Lindelof hypothesis $\mu(1/2)=0$, implied by RH. Iwaniec-Kowalski Ch. 5. Lean status none.


### number-theory.selberg-delange-method

- **title**: The Selberg-Delange Method
- **unit**: `21.11.05`
- **prerequisites**: `21.11.04` (Perron's formula and Mellin inversion — the truncated-contour engine), `06.01.16` (Riemann zeta function — the model singular factor $\zeta(s)^z$), `06.01.15` (Gamma function — the Hankel-loop reciprocal $1/\Gamma(z)$)
- **notes**: stub - see unit `21.11.05`. Mean values of multiplicative functions whose Dirichlet series factors as $F(s)=\zeta(s)^z G(s)$ with $G$ holomorphic and non-vanishing at $s=1$ and $z\in\mathbb{C}$: the simple pole of $\zeta$ at $s=1$ becomes a branch point of exponent $-z$, and deforming the Perron contour onto a Hankel loop around the cut $(-\infty,1]$ extracts $\sum_{n\le x} f(n) \sim \frac{G(1)}{\Gamma(z)}\,x\,(\log x)^{z-1}$, the constant being the Hankel-loop integral $1/\Gamma(z)$. Full power-of-$\log x$ expansion $\sum_j a_j/\Gamma(z-j)\,(\log x)^{z-1-j}$; specialises to residue calculus at integer $z$ ($z=2$ gives Dirichlet's $\sum d(n)\sim x\log x$). Applications: the Landau-Selberg-Sathe theorem $\pi_k(x)\sim \frac{x}{\log x}\frac{(\log\log x)^{k-1}}{(k-1)!}$ for integers with exactly $k$ prime factors; the Landau-Ramanujan law $\#\{n\le x: n=a^2+b^2\}\sim Kx/\sqrt{\log x}$ ($z=1/2$); the moment generating function $\sum_{n\le x} z^{\omega(n)}$ underlying the Erd\H{o}s-Kac central limit theorem. Selberg 1954 + Delange 1954; Tenenbaum \S II.5-6, Montgomery-Vaughan \S II.5. Lean status none.


### number-theory.partition-function-pentagonal-number-theorem

- **title**: The Partition Function, Generating Functions, and the Pentagonal Number Theorem
- **unit**: `21.16.01`
- **prerequisites**: `21.11.01` (arithmetic functions, Dirichlet convolution, Mobius inversion: the inversion-in-a-convolution-ring template), `02.03.03` (infinite series and convergence tests: absolute convergence of the generating product on $|q|<1$)
- **notes**: stub - see unit `21.16.01`. The partition function $p(n)$ and its generating function $\sum p(n)q^n = \prod(1-q^n)^{-1}$; restricted partitions (distinct/odd parts, Euler's identity; Glaisher's theorem $\prod(1+q^k+\cdots+q^{(d-1)k}) = \prod_{d\nmid k}(1-q^k)^{-1}$); Euler's pentagonal number theorem $\prod(1-q^n) = \sum_k (-1)^k q^{k(3k-1)/2}$ with Franklin's sign-reversing involution proof, and the resulting $O(\sqrt n)$ recurrence for $p(n)$; the Jacobi triple product $\prod(1-q^{2m})(1+q^{2m-1}z)(1+q^{2m-1}z^{-1}) = \sum_k q^{k^2}z^k$ as the parent identity; the Dedekind-eta / modular viewpoint $\prod(1-q^k) = q^{-1/24}\eta(\tau)$; the Hardy-Ramanujan asymptotic $p(n)\sim e^{\pi\sqrt{2n/3}}/(4n\sqrt3)$ (circle-method prelude) and Rademacher's exact series; Ramanujan congruences $p(5n+4)\equiv0\,(5)$, $p(7n+5)\equiv0\,(7)$, $p(11n+6)\equiv0\,(11)$ stated; Rogers-Ramanujan identities. Apostol Ch. 14; Andrews *Theory of Partitions*. Cross-ref 21.04.04 for the theta-series specialisation. Lean status none.


### number-theory.weyl-sums-differencing-equidistribution

- **title**: Weyl Sums, Weyl Differencing, and Equidistribution
- **unit**: `21.15.02`
- **prerequisites**: `21.15.01` (Poisson summation: the transform/completion engine and the van der Corput $B$-process), `02.10.04` (Fourier transform and Plancherel: the additive characters $e(\cdot)$ on $\mathbb{R}/\mathbb{Z}$)
- **notes**: stub - see unit `21.15.02`. Weyl sums $S_N(f) = \sum_{n\le N} e(f(n))$ for a polynomial phase $f$; **Weyl's criterion** ($(x_n)$ equidistributed mod $1$ iff $\frac1N\sum_{n\le N} e(h x_n)\to 0$ for every integer $h\ne 0$); the **Weyl differencing inequality** $|S|^2 \le N\sum_{|h|<N}|\sum_n e(\Delta_h f(n))|$ reducing a degree-$k$ sum to averages of degree-$(k-1)$ sums via the forward difference $\Delta_h f(n)=f(n+h)-f(n)$; equidistribution of $(\alpha n^k)$ for irrational $\alpha$ by induction on degree; **Weyl's inequality** with saving exponent $2^{1-k}$ and the major/minor-arc dichotomy of the circle method; **van der Corput's difference theorem** and the **Erdős-Turán** discrepancy inequality. Weyl 1916; Iwaniec-Kowalski Ch. 8. Lean status none: Mathlib lacks the general Weyl criterion, the differencing inequality, the polynomial Weyl bound, van der Corput's difference theorem, and the Erdős-Turán inequality.


### number-theory.average-orders-summation-toolkit

- **title**: Average Orders of Arithmetic Functions and the Summation Toolkit
- **unit**: `21.11.02`
- **prerequisites**: `21.11.01` (arithmetic functions, Dirichlet convolution, Mobius), `02.03.03` (infinite series convergence tests)
- **notes**: stub - see unit `21.11.02`. The analytic-NT summation toolkit: Abel/partial summation $\sum_{y<n\le x} a(n)\phi(n) = A(x)\phi(x)-A(y)\phi(y)-\int_y^x A(t)\phi'(t)dt$, the Euler-Maclaurin (Euler summation) formula with Bernoulli-polynomial remainder, and the Dirichlet hyperbola method $\sum_{n\le x}(f*g)(n)$ split at $\sqrt x$. Average orders: $\sum_{n\le x} d(n)=x\log x+(2\gamma-1)x+O(\sqrt x)$ (the Dirichlet divisor problem; error $\Delta(x)$, exponent $\theta\in[1/4,\,0.3149]$, Hardy $\Omega(x^{1/4})$), $\sum_{n\le x}\sigma(n)=\frac{\pi^2}{12}x^2+O(x\log x)$, $\sum_{n\le x}\varphi(n)=\frac{3}{\pi^2}x^2+O(x\log x)$ (coprime-pair density $6/\pi^2$), $\sum_{n\le x}1/n=\log x+\gamma+O(1/x)$. The Dirichlet-series $\leftrightarrow$ summatory-function dictionary $D(f;s)=s\int_1^\infty F(x)x^{-s-1}dx$. The $\mu$ mean value $M(x)=o(x)$ marks the elementary boundary (equivalent to PNT). Apostol Ch. 3; Montgomery-Vaughan §2. Lean status none.


### number-theory.kloosterman-sums-kuznetsov-formula

- **title**: Kloosterman Sums and the Kuznetsov Spectral Formula
- **unit**: `21.14.05`
- **prerequisites**: `21.15.04` (Kloosterman sums and the Weil bound), `21.04.01` (modular forms on SL_2(Z)), `21.04.02` (Hecke operators)
- **notes**: stub - see unit `21.14.05`. Sums of Kloosterman sums $\sum_c S(m,n;c)/c\,\varphi(4\pi\sqrt{mn}/c)$ and their cancellation beyond the Weil bound. The Petersson trace formula (holomorphic cusp forms, kernel $J_{k-1}$) and the Kuznetsov-Bruggeman formula (Maass forms, Bessel/Selberg-Harish-Chandra transform pair) equate the weighted Kloosterman geometric side to the spectral side: Fourier coefficients of an orthonormal cusp-form basis plus the Eisenstein continuous spectrum, with rigid diagonal main term $\delta_{mn}$. The Selberg eigenvalue conjecture $\lambda_1\ge 1/4$ (record $975/4096\approx 0.238$, Kim-Sarnak) and the Linnik-Selberg conjecture on Kloosterman-sum cancellation ($X^\varepsilon$ vs Kuznetsov's $X^{1/6+\varepsilon}$). Applications: equidistribution of Kloosterman fractions $\bar x/c$ via Weyl's criterion, the Deshouillers-Iwaniec spectral large sieve, subconvexity of $L$-functions by amplification. Iwaniec-Kowalski Ch. 16; Iwaniec, Spectral Methods. Lean status none.


### number-theory.chebyshev-bounds-bertrand-mertens

- **title**: Chebyshev's Bounds, Bertrand's Postulate, and Mertens' Theorems
- **unit**: `21.11.03`
- **prerequisites**: `21.11.01` (arithmetic functions, Dirichlet convolution, $\log = \Lambda * \mathbf{1}$), `21.01.02` (primes, fundamental theorem of arithmetic, $\pi(x)$)
- **notes**: stub - see unit `21.11.03`. The elementary (complex-analysis-free) prime-counting estimates of Apostol Ch. 4, preceding the analytic PNT. The **Chebyshev functions** $\vartheta(x)=\sum_{p\leq x}\log p$ and $\psi(x)=\sum_{n\leq x}\Lambda(n)=\sum_{m\geq 1}\vartheta(x^{1/m})$, with $\psi-\vartheta=O(\sqrt x\log x)$. **Chebyshev's bounds** $c_1 x\leq\psi(x)\leq c_2 x$ ($c_1=\log 2$, $c_2=2\log 2$), proved from the factorial identity $\log N!=\sum_{n\leq N}\Lambda(n)\lfloor N/n\rfloor$ (a partial summation of $\log=\Lambda*\mathbf{1}$) and the central binomial coefficient $\binom{2N}{N}\geq 4^N/(2N+1)$ with $\log\binom{2N}{N}\leq\psi(2N)$. The **equivalence** $\psi(x)\sim x\iff\vartheta(x)\sim x\iff\pi(x)\sim x/\log x\iff$ PNT (by Abel summation), reducing PNT to $\psi(x)\sim x$; the constant-$1$ step is beyond the elementary band (Chebyshev's straddling constants $0.92129$ and $1.10555$) and needs [21.12.01]. **Bertrand's postulate** (a prime in $(n,2n]$ for every $n\geq 1$): Erdős 1932 via the factorisation of $\binom{2n}{n}$ — exponents $p^{r_p}\leq 2n$, no prime factors in $(2n/3,n]$, primorial bound $\prod_{p\leq m}p<4^m$. **Mertens' theorems**: (i) $\sum_{p\leq x}(\log p)/p=\log x+O(1)$ from $\log\lfloor x\rfloor!=x\log x-x+O(\log x)$ and $\psi(x)=O(x)$; (ii) $\sum_{p\leq x}1/p=\log\log x+M+o(1)$ ($M=0.2614972128\ldots$ Meissel-Mertens) by Abel summation; (iii) $\prod_{p\leq x}(1-1/p)^{-1}\sim e^{\gamma}\log x$ ($\gamma$ Euler-Mascheroni), the sieve-theory prime-product backbone. Shapiro's Tauberian theorem abstracts the $\lfloor x/n\rfloor\to 1/n$ upgrade. Originators Chebyshev 1852, Mertens 1874, Erdős 1932. Lean status none: Mathlib has `vonMangoldt`, `Nat.bertrand`, and the central-binomial bounds, but no single module proving the two-sided $\psi$ bound, the $\vartheta\sim x\iff\pi\sim x/\log x$ equivalence, and the three Mertens theorems with $M$ and $e^{\gamma}$ end to end.


### number-theory.van-der-corput-method-exponential-sums

- **title**: van der Corput's Method for Exponential Sums
- **unit**: `21.15.03`
- **prerequisites**: `21.15.02` (Weyl sums and Weyl differencing), `21.15.01` (Poisson and Voronoi summation), `02.20.05` (oscillatory integrals and stationary phase)
- **notes**: stub - see unit `21.15.03`. Van der Corput's method for $\sum_n e(f(n))$ with a smooth non-polynomial phase: the **$A$-process** (Weyl-van der Corput differencing inequality with a free shift length $H$, reducing to averaged shorter difference sums) and the **$B$-process** (Poisson summation plus stationary phase, replacing the sum by its Legendre-dual sum over slopes $m=f'(x_m)$ with weight $|f''(x_m)|^{-1/2}$). The **exponent pair** formalism $(\kappa,\lambda)$ with $\sum_{N<n\le 2N} e(f(n))\ll (F/N)^\kappa N^\lambda$, the operators $A(\kappa,\lambda)=(\tfrac{\kappa}{2\kappa+2},\tfrac{\kappa+\lambda+1}{2\kappa+2})$ and $B(\kappa,\lambda)=(\lambda-\tfrac12,\kappa+\tfrac12)$ ($B$ an involution), and the $AB$-closure of the trivial pair $(0,1)$. Applications: the convexity-breaking bound $\zeta(1/2+it)\ll t^{1/6+\varepsilon}$ from $(\tfrac16,\tfrac23)=AB(0,1)$, and the $x^{1/3}$ exponents for the Dirichlet divisor error $\Delta(x)$ and the Gauss circle error $P(x)$ via the phase $2\sqrt{xn}$. The sum analogue of the integral van der Corput lemma in `02.20.05`. Iwaniec-Kowalski Ch. 8; Graham-Kolesnik. Lean status none.

### number-theory.von-mangoldt-psi-zeta-logarithmic-derivative

- **title**: The von Mangoldt Function, the Chebyshev Psi Function, and the Logarithmic Derivative of Zeta
- **unit**: `21.12.01`
- **prerequisites**: `21.11.03` (Chebyshev $\psi$, $\vartheta$, $\log=\Lambda*\mathbf{1}$, elementary bounds), `21.03.01` (Riemann zeta, Euler product), `06.01.16` (zeta analytic continuation, functional equation, pole/zeros)
- **notes**: stub - see unit `21.12.01`. The analytic bridge from elementary Chebyshev bounds to the analytic PNT (Davenport §12). The **von Mangoldt function** $\Lambda(n)=\log p$ on prime powers $p^m$ and the identity $-\zeta'(s)/\zeta(s)=\sum_n\Lambda(n)n^{-s}$ for $\operatorname{Re}(s)>1$, obtained as the logarithmic derivative of the Euler product. The **Chebyshev psi** $\psi(x)=\sum_{n\leq x}\Lambda(n)$ as the natural analytic prime-counting weight ($\psi(x)\sim x\iff$ PNT). The analytic structure of $-\zeta'/\zeta$: simple pole at $s=1$ with residue $+1$ (the main term $x$), poles at each non-trivial zero $\rho$ with residue $-m$ (multiplicity), and trivial zeros. The **Hadamard product** for $\xi(s)$ and the **partial-fraction expansion** $\zeta'/\zeta=B-1/(s-1)+\sum_\rho(1/(s-\rho)+1/\rho)-\tfrac12\log\pi+\tfrac12\Gamma'/\Gamma(s/2+1)$. The **von Mangoldt explicit formula** $\psi(x)=x-\sum_\rho x^\rho/\rho-\log(2\pi)-\tfrac12\log(1-x^{-2})$ via Perron's formula and a contour shift, feeding the PNT contour proof. PNT $\iff\zeta(1+it)\neq 0$. Originators Riemann 1859, von Mangoldt 1895, Hadamard / de la Vallée Poussin 1896. Lean status none: Mathlib has `vonMangoldt`, `LSeries.vonMangoldt` ($=-\zeta'/\zeta$), and a zero-free line, but no single module assembling the Hadamard product, the partial-fraction expansion, and the explicit formula end to end.


### number-theory.mean-values-multiplicative-halasz-wirsing

- **title**: Mean Values of Multiplicative Functions: Halász's Theorem
- **unit**: `21.14.03`
- **prerequisites**: `21.11.02` (average orders and the summation toolkit — partial summation, Mertens' estimates, the Dirichlet-series/summatory-function pairing), `21.11.05` (the Selberg-Delange method — the contour-method comparison point)
- **notes**: stub - see unit `21.14.03`. Mean values of multiplicative $f$ with $|f|\le 1$. **Halász's theorem**: $\frac1x|\sum_{n\le x}f(n)|\ll(1+M)e^{-M}+1/T$ with $M=\min_{|t|\le T}\sum_{p\le x}(1-\operatorname{Re}f(p)p^{-it})/p$ the minimal **pretentious distance** to the family $n^{it}$, giving the dichotomy that the mean decays unless $f$ pretends to be some $n^{it}$. **Wirsing's theorem** upgrades the bound to a genuine asymptotic $\frac1x\sum_{n\le x}f(n)\sim\frac{e^{-\gamma\kappa}}{\Gamma(\kappa)}\prod_{p\le x}(1-1/p)^\kappa(1+f(p)/p+\cdots)$ for real non-negative $f$ of prime-averaged density $\kappa$ (resolves Erdős-Wintner), coinciding with Selberg-Delange on the factored Euler product. The **pretentious distance** $\mathbb{D}(f,g;x)^2=\sum_{p\le x}(1-\operatorname{Re}f(p)\overline{g(p)})/p$ is a pseudometric obeying the triangle inequality (Granville-Soundararajan). The **Halász-Montgomery inequality** $\sum_r|\sum_n a_n n^{-it_r}|^2\le(N+R\Delta^{-1})\sum_n|a_n|^2$ transmits pointwise control of $F(s)$ on $\operatorname{Re}s=1$ to the mean and is the additive **large sieve** specialised to logarithmic frequencies. Delange's 1961 theorem pins the surviving case ($\sum_p(1-f(p))/p$ convergent). Soft/real-variable counterpart to the Selberg-Delange contour method of 21.11.05. Halász 1968 + Wirsing 1967 + Granville-Soundararajan 2007; Montgomery-Vaughan §6, Tenenbaum §III.4. Lean status none.


### number-theory.hardy-ramanujan-rademacher-circle-method

- **title**: The Hardy-Ramanujan-Rademacher Asymptotics via the Circle Method
- **unit**: `21.16.02`
- **prerequisites**: `21.16.01` (partition function, generating function, pentagonal number theorem), `21.15.01` (Poisson/Voronoi summation), `06.01.15` (Gamma function)
- **notes**: stub - see unit `21.16.02`. The circle method: $p(n)=\frac{1}{2\pi i}\oint F(q)q^{-n-1}dq$ on a circle approaching the unit circle, Farey dissection into major arcs about each $e^{2\pi i h/k}$, the modular transformation of $F=1/(q;q)_\infty=q^{1/24}/\eta(\tau)$ via $\eta(-1/\tau)=\sqrt{-i\tau}\,\eta(\tau)$ and the Dedekind sums $s(h,k)$. The Hardy-Ramanujan asymptotic $p(n)\sim e^{\pi\sqrt{2n/3}}/(4n\sqrt3)$ by saddle point at $z_0=(24n)^{-1/2}$, $g(z_0)=\pi\sqrt{2n/3}$. Rademacher's exact convergent series with Kloosterman-type sums $A_k(n)=\sum_h\omega_{h,k}e^{-2\pi inh/k}$ and the Bessel function $I_{3/2}$ (Ford circles). The general Hardy-Littlewood method (Waring, ternary Goldbach): major/minor arcs, singular series $\mathfrak{S}(n)$, Weyl/Vinogradov minor-arc bounds. Apostol GTM 41 Ch. 3, 5; Hardy-Ramanujan 1918; Rademacher 1937; Vaughan 1997. Lean status none.

### number-theory.large-sieve-inequality-brun-titchmarsh

- **title**: The Large Sieve Inequality and Brun-Titchmarsh
- **unit**: `21.14.01`
- **prerequisites**: `21.11.02` (average orders and the summation toolkit), `21.15.01` (Poisson and Voronoi summation), `02.10.04` (Fourier transform and Plancherel)
- **notes**: stub - see unit `21.14.01`. The large sieve inequality $\sum_r |S(\alpha_r)|^2 \le (N+\delta^{-1})\sum_n |a_n|^2$ for $\delta$-spaced points, optimal constant $N+Q^2$ for Farey points of order $Q$, proved by the duality principle plus a Beurling-Selberg band-limited majorant. The arithmetic large sieve $\sum_{q\le Q}\sum_{a\,(q)}^* |S(a/q)|^2 \le (N+Q^2)\sum_n |a_n|^2$ and its multiplicative (Dirichlet-character) form via Gauss-sum separation. Sifted-set bound $|\mathcal{S}| \le (N+Q^2)/L(Q)$ with local densities $\omega(p)/(p-\omega(p))$; the Brun-Titchmarsh theorem $\pi(x;q,a)\le 2x/(\varphi(q)\log(x/q))$ uniform in $q<x$, the parity barrier blocking the constant $1+o(1)$. Preview of the Bombieri-Vinogradov theorem as an averaged GRH. Montgomery-Vaughan §7; Linnik 1941, Bombieri 1965, Montgomery-Vaughan 1973. Lean status none.


### number-theory.combinatorial-sieve-brun-selberg

- **title**: Combinatorial Sieve Methods: Brun and Selberg
- **unit**: `21.14.04`
- **prerequisites**: `21.11.01` (arithmetic functions, Dirichlet convolution, Möbius inversion $\mathbf{1}_{n=1}=\sum_{d\mid n}\mu(d)$), `21.11.03` (Chebyshev bounds, Mertens products $\prod_{p<z}(1-1/p)\sim e^{-\gamma}/\log z$)
- **notes**: stub - see unit `21.14.04`. The **sieve problem**: a sequence $\mathcal{A}=(a_n)$ of mass $X$, a prime set $\mathcal{P}$, sifting level $z$, $P(z)=\prod_{p<z}p$, and the sifting function $S(\mathcal{A},\mathcal{P},z)=\sum_{(n,P(z))=1}a_n$ with density $A_d=g(d)X+r_d$ and $V(z)=\prod_{p<z}(1-g(p))$. The **Legendre sieve** $S=XV(z)+\sum_{d\mid P(z)}\mu(d)r_d$ is exact but inert: $2^{\pi(z)}$ remainder terms swamp the main term (error accumulation). **Brun's pure / combinatorial sieve** truncates the Möbius sum at $\omega(d)\le 2k$, two-sided by the Bonferroni inequalities $\sum_{j\le m}\binom{r}{j}(-1)^j=(-1)^m\binom{r-1}{m}$; the **fundamental lemma** gives $S=XV(z)(1+O(e^{-s\log s+O(s)}))$, $s=\log X/\log z$. The **Selberg $\Lambda^2$ upper-bound sieve** optimises $(\sum_{d\mid n}\lambda_d)^2\ge\mathbf{1}_{n=1}$ with $\lambda_1=1$: the quadratic form $Q(\lambda)=\sum_{d_1,d_2}\lambda_{d_1}\lambda_{d_2}g([d_1,d_2])$ diagonalises to $\sum_e h(e)y_e^2$ ($h(p)=g(p)/(1-g(p))$), minimum $1/G(z)$, $G(z)=\sum_{d<z}h(d)$, by Cauchy-Schwarz. **Sieve dimension** $\kappa$: $V(z)\asymp(\log z)^{-\kappa}$; linear sieve $\kappa=1$ (Jurkat-Richert $F,f$, $s>2$ for positive lower bound), twin-prime/Goldbach $\kappa=2$. Applications: **Brun's theorem** ($\sum 1/p$ over twin primes converges to Brun's constant $\approx 1.902$), $\pi_2(x)\ll x(\log\log x)^2/(\log x)^2$, Goldbach upper bound $r(N)\ll\mathfrak{S}(N)N/(\log N)^2$, **Chen's theorem** ($p+P_2$, stated). The **parity problem** (Selberg): a combinatorial sieve cannot distinguish $\Omega$-even from $\Omega$-odd integers (blind to the Liouville function $\lambda(n)$), so it cannot isolate primes; evaded by bilinear/type-II sums (Friedlander-Iwaniec $a^2+b^4$). Originators Brun 1919/1920, Selberg 1947, Halberstam-Richert 1974, Chen 1973, Friedlander-Iwaniec 2010. Iwaniec-Kowalski Ch. 6. Lean status none: Mathlib has no `Sieve` namespace at all (Möbius, squarefree, finset inclusion-exclusion exist, but not the sifting function, Bonferroni/Brun truncation, fundamental lemma, or Selberg diagonalisation).


### number-theory.effective-zero-free-region-error-term

- **title**: Effective Zero-Free Regions for Zeta and the Prime Number Theorem Error Term
- **unit**: `21.12.03`
- **prerequisites**: `21.12.01` (von Mangoldt $\Lambda$, $-\zeta'/\zeta=\sum\Lambda(n)n^{-s}$, partial-fraction expansion, explicit formula), `06.01.16` (zeta analytic continuation, functional equation, boundary nonvanishing $\zeta(1+it)\neq 0$)
- **notes**: stub - see unit `21.12.03`. Sharpens the boundary nonvanishing of [06.01.16] into an effective region (Davenport §13-18). The **classical de la Vallee Poussin region** $\sigma>1-c/\log(|t|+2)$ from the inequality $3+4\cos\theta+\cos 2\theta=2(1+\cos\theta)^2\geq 0$ weighting $-\zeta'/\zeta$ at heights $0,t,2t$, plus partial-fraction bounds on $\zeta'/\zeta$ (Borel-Caratheodory + Hadamard three-circles on the log-derivative). The resulting **PNT error term** $\psi(x)=x+O(x\exp(-c\sqrt{\log x}))$ via a Perron contour shift to the region boundary, with $T=\exp(\sqrt{\log x})$ the optimal truncation height. The **Vinogradov-Korobov** region $\sigma>1-c/(\log t)^{2/3}(\log\log t)^{1/3}$ via van der Corput / Vinogradov exponential-sum bounds (error exponent $3/5$). Connection to RH: $\beta\leq\Theta$ for all zeros $\iff\psi(x)-x=O(x^{\Theta+\varepsilon})$, so RH $\iff O(x^{1/2}\log^2 x)$. **Zero-density estimates** $N(\sigma,T)\ll T^{A(1-\sigma)}\log^B T$ as an RH substitute. Originators de la Vallee Poussin 1899, Korobov / Vinogradov 1958; Titchmarsh (rev. Heath-Brown), Montgomery-Vaughan. Lean status none: Mathlib has the boundary nonvanishing `riemannZeta_ne_zero_of_one_le_re` but no positive-width region with an explicit constant, no Borel-Caratheodory/three-circles log-derivative bounds, no exponential-sum machinery, and no contour-shift error term.


### number-theory.prime-number-theorem-contour-integration

- **title**: The Prime Number Theorem via Contour Integration
- **unit**: `21.12.02`
- **prerequisites**: `21.12.01` (von Mangoldt $\Lambda$, $\psi(x)$, $-\zeta'/\zeta=\sum\Lambda(n)n^{-s}$, explicit formula), `21.11.04` (truncated Perron formula, discontinuous integral), `06.01.16` (zeta continuation, functional equation, $\zeta(1+it)\neq 0$)
- **notes**: stub - see unit `21.12.02`. The full analytic proof of the **prime number theorem** $\psi(x)\sim x$ (equivalently $\pi(x)\sim x/\log x$, $\pi(x)\sim\operatorname{Li}(x)$) by contour integration (Davenport §17-18). Feed $-\zeta'/\zeta$ into the **truncated Perron formula** of `21.11.04`, shift the contour left past the simple pole at $s=1$ (residue $x$, the main term), and bound the four contour segments using the **de la Vallée Poussin zero-free region** $\sigma>1-c/\log(|t|+2)$ — derived from the **$3+4\cos\theta+\cos2\theta=2(1+\cos\theta)^2\ge 0$** inequality forcing $\zeta(1+it)\neq 0$ — together with $|\zeta'/\zeta|\ll\log^2(|t|+2)$ on its boundary. Optimising $\log T\asymp\sqrt{\log x}$ balances the truncation error against the shifted-line term and yields $\psi(x)=x+O(x\exp(-c\sqrt{\log x}))$. Equivalence of the PNT forms via $\psi-\vartheta=O(\sqrt x\log^2 x)$ and Abel summation. The **Newman / Wiener-Ikehara Tauberian** alternative: boundary non-vanishing $\zeta(1+it)\neq 0$ plus Newman's analytic lemma give $\psi(x)\sim x$ without a region (no error term). Vinogradov-Korobov region and RH sharpen the error to $x^{1/2}\log^2 x$. Originators Hadamard / de la Vallée Poussin 1896, Mertens 1898 (the $3+4\cos\theta+\cos2\theta$ trick), Newman 1980, Korevaar 1982. Lean status none: the Newman-Tauberian PNT is in the `PrimeNumberTheoremAnd` project, but the classical de la Vallée Poussin contour proof with the quantitative zero-free region and error term is not packaged in Mathlib proper.

### number-theory.vinogradov-mean-value-theorem

- **title**: The Vinogradov Mean Value Theorem
- **unit**: `21.15.05`
- **prerequisites**: `21.15.03` (van der Corput's method, the smooth-phase exponent-pair calculus that Vinogradov surpasses for high degree), `21.15.02` (Weyl sums and Weyl differencing, whose lossy $2^{1-k}$ saving the mean value replaces)
- **notes**: stub - see unit `21.15.05`. The **Vinogradov mean value** $J_{s,k}(N)$ counts integer solutions of $\sum_{i\le s} x_i^j=\sum_{i\le s} y_i^j$ ($1\le j\le k$), $1\le x_i,y_i\le N$; by orthogonality $J_{s,k}(N)=\int_{\mathbb T^k}|\sum_{n\le N}e(\alpha_1 n+\cdots+\alpha_k n^k)|^{2s}\,d\boldsymbol\alpha$. Two forced floors: **diagonal** ($\mathbf y$ a permutation of $\mathbf x$) gives $\gg N^s$, dimension-counting gives $\gg N^{2s-k(k+1)/2}$. The **main conjecture** $J_{s,k}(N)\ll_\varepsilon N^{s+\varepsilon}+N^{2s-k(k+1)/2+\varepsilon}$, with all difficulty at the **critical exponent** $s_k=k(k+1)/2$ (Exercise 6: critical case implies the rest by trivial summation). **Resolution** (2015-2016): **Bourgain-Demeter-Guth 2016** via $\ell^2$ **decoupling** for the moment curve $\Gamma_k=\{(t,\ldots,t^k)\}$ (multilinear Kakeya + Bourgain-Guth induction on scales + parabolic rescaling), endpoint $p=k(k+1)$; **Wooley 2012/2019** via **efficient congruencing** ($p$-adic Vandermonde divisibility, nested form for all $k$). Both read the **Vandermonde rigidity** (equal power sums $\Rightarrow$ equal multiset, via Newton's identities) as the one geometric fact — curvature of $\Gamma_k$ archimedean, $p$-adic divisibility non-archimedean. Consequences: sharpest **Weyl-sum bounds** $N^{1-\rho(k)+\varepsilon}$, $\rho(k)\asymp 1/(k^2\log k)$ (beats van der Corput for high $k$); **Vinogradov-Korobov zero-free region** $\sigma\ge 1-c/(\log|t|)^{2/3}(\log\log|t|)^{1/3}$ for $\zeta$; **Waring's problem** $G(k)\le k(\log k+\log\log k+O(1))$. Originators Vinogradov 1935, Linnik, Karatsuba; resolution Wooley, Bourgain-Demeter-Guth. Iwaniec-Kowalski Ch. 8, Demeter 2020. Lean status none: Mathlib has the orthogonality primitives but no Vinogradov system, no efficient-congruencing iteration, and no decoupling apparatus (not even the parabola $k=2$ case).


### number-theory.zero-free-regions-l-functions-siegel-zeros

- **title**: Zero-Free Regions for Dirichlet L-Functions and Exceptional (Siegel) Zeros
- **unit**: `21.13.01`
- **prerequisites**: `21.12.01` (von Mangoldt $\Lambda$, the identity $-\zeta'/\zeta=\sum\Lambda(n)n^{-s}$, the $3+4\cos\theta+\cos2\theta$ device), `21.03.02` (Dirichlet characters, $L(s,\chi)$, Euler product, $L(1,\chi)\neq0$), `06.01.16` (zeta analytic continuation, functional equation, Hadamard zeros)
- **notes**: stub - see unit `21.13.01`. The de la Vallée Poussin zero-free region $\sigma>1-c/\log(q(|t|+2))$ for $L(s,\chi)$, obtained by applying $3+4\cos\theta+\cos2\theta=2(1+\cos\theta)^2\geq0$ to $L(s,\chi_0)^3L(s+it,\chi)^4L(s+2it,\chi^2)$ (Davenport §14). The **complication for real characters**: when $\chi^2=\chi_0$ the third factor gains a second pole at $s=1$, the pole budget balances, and the method cannot exclude a single real **exceptional / Siegel zero** $\beta$ near $1$. **Landau's repulsion** (the product $\zeta L(\cdot,\chi_1)L(\cdot,\chi_2)L(\cdot,\chi_1\chi_2)$ has non-negative coefficients, so distinct real characters have at most one near-line zero; at most one exceptional modulus per dyadic range). **Page's bound** $\beta<1-c/(\sqrt q\log^2 q)$ (effective, feeble) versus **Siegel's bound** $L(1,\chi)\gg_\varepsilon q^{-\varepsilon}$, i.e. $1-\beta\gg_\varepsilon q^{-\varepsilon}$ (strong, ineffective; proved in 21.13.02). Class-number link: $h(D)\gg_\varepsilon|D|^{1/2-\varepsilon}$ for imaginary quadratic fields via Dirichlet's class-number formula; the Deuring-Heilbronn phenomenon. The deep obstruction underlying PNT-in-AP uniformity (Siegel-Walfisz, 21.13.03). Effective $\zeta$ region (21.12.03) is the $q=1$ specialisation. Originators de la Vallée Poussin 1896, Landau 1918, Page 1935, Siegel 1935. Lean status none: Mathlib has the continued $L$-functions, functional equation, and line non-vanishing, but not the quantitative zero-free region, the exceptional-zero dichotomy, the repulsion theorem, or the ineffective Siegel bound.

### number-theory.riemann-von-mangoldt-explicit-formula

- **title**: The Riemann-von Mangoldt Explicit Formula
- **unit**: `21.12.04`
- **prerequisites**: `21.12.02` (PNT contour proof, Perron integral of $-\zeta'/\zeta$, residue at $s=1$), `21.12.01` (von Mangoldt $\Lambda$, $\psi(x)$, partial-fraction expansion of $\zeta'/\zeta$, Hadamard product), `06.01.17` (Weierstrass-Hadamard factorisation of entire functions)
- **notes**: stub - see unit `21.12.04`. The **Riemann-von Mangoldt explicit formula** $\psi(x) = x - \sum_\rho x^\rho/\rho - \log(2\pi) - \tfrac12\log(1 - x^{-2})$, the sum over the critical zeros $\rho$, derived via the **truncated Perron integral** of $-\zeta'/\zeta$ and summation of residues (pole at $s=1$ giving $x$, each zero giving $-x^\rho/\rho$, $s=0$ giving $-\log 2\pi$, elementary zeros $s=-2k$ giving $-\tfrac12\log(1-x^{-2})$). The **truncated explicit formula** $\psi(x) = x - \sum_{|\gamma|\le T} x^\rho/\rho + O(x\log^2(xT)/T + \log x)$ for estimates. The **Riemann-von Mangoldt zero-counting formula** $N(T) = (T/2\pi)\log(T/2\pi) - T/2\pi + O(\log T)$ via the argument principle on the completed $\xi$ (Stirling smooth part plus $S(T)=\pi^{-1}\arg\zeta(\tfrac12+iT)=O(\log T)$). The **primes-to-zeros duality** (oscillation of $\psi$ governed by the zeros, conditionally convergent zero-sum paired $\rho,\bar\rho$), and Riemann's prime-counting $J(x) = \operatorname{Li}(x) - \sum_\rho\operatorname{Li}(x^\rho) - \log 2 + \cdots$ with $\pi(x)=\sum_m \mu(m)/m\, J(x^{1/m})$ and the bias $-\tfrac12\operatorname{Li}(\sqrt x)$. Weil's reciprocal generalisation. Davenport §17, Riemann 1859, von Mangoldt 1895, Backlund 1918. Lean status none: the Newman-Tauberian PNT is in `PrimeNumberTheoremAnd`, but the explicit formula with its conditionally convergent zero-sum, the truncated remainder, and the argument-principle $N(T)$ are not packaged in Mathlib proper.

### number-theory.siegel-theorem-exceptional-zero

- **title**: Siegel's Theorem on the Exceptional Zero
- **unit**: `21.13.02`
- **prerequisites**: `21.13.01` (exceptional-zero dichotomy, auxiliary product, Landau repulsion, Page's bound), `21.03.02` (Dirichlet characters, $L(s,\chi)$, $L(1,\chi)\neq0$, class-number formula)
- **notes**: stub - see unit `21.13.02`. Siegel's theorem: for every $\varepsilon>0$ there is $c(\varepsilon)>0$ with $L(1,\chi)>c(\varepsilon)q^{-\varepsilon}$ for real primitive $\chi$ mod $q$, equivalently $\beta<1-c(\varepsilon)q^{-\varepsilon}$ (Davenport §21). Proof via the auxiliary product of two $L$-functions $\zeta(s)L(s,\chi_1)L(s,\chi_2)L(s,\chi_1\chi_2)$ with non-negative coefficients, and the **dichotomy** on whether some real character has a Siegel zero: either it is a fixed lever bounding every other $L(1,\chi_2)$, or no such zero exists and the bound is direct. **Ineffectivity** for $\varepsilon<1/2$: the constant depends on the value of a hypothesised zero that cannot be exhibited, or on certifying no such zero exists (the open Landau-Siegel problem). Consequence: **Siegel's class-number bound** $h(-d)\gg_\varepsilon d^{1/2-\varepsilon}$ via Dirichlet's formula $h(-d)=w\sqrt{d}\,L(1,\chi_{-d})/(2\pi)$, settling Gauss's conjecture ineffectively. **Brauer-Siegel** $\log(h_K R_K)/\log\sqrt{|d_K|}\to1$ (imaginary-quadratic case, regulator $1$, recovers Siegel). Contrast with the **effective Goldfeld-Gross-Zagier** bound $h(-d)\gg(\log d)^{1-\varepsilon}$, effective because a known elliptic-curve $L$-function with a triple central zero replaces the undecided case. Class-number-one (nine Heegner discriminants) needed Heegner/Baker/Stark. Lean status none: Mathlib lacks the auxiliary-product argument, the ineffective Siegel bound, and the class-number formula link.


### number-theory.prime-number-theorem-arithmetic-progressions-siegel-walfisz

- **title**: The Prime Number Theorem in Arithmetic Progressions and Siegel-Walfisz
- **unit**: `21.13.03`
- **prerequisites**: `21.13.01` (zero-free region for $L(s,\chi)$, Landau repulsion, the isolated exceptional/Siegel zero $\beta$), `21.12.02` (the contour proof of the PNT, truncated Perron, the optimisation $\log T=\sqrt{\log x}$), `21.03.02` (Dirichlet characters, orthogonality, $L(1,\chi)\neq0$)
- **notes**: stub - see unit `21.13.03`. The **PNT in arithmetic progressions** $\psi(x;q,a)\sim x/\varphi(q)$ for $(a,q)=1$ via the exact **orthogonality decomposition** $\psi(x;q,a)=\varphi(q)^{-1}\sum_{\chi}\overline\chi(a)\psi(x,\chi)$ (Davenport §19-22). Each twisted $\psi(x,\chi)=-\sum_\rho x^\rho/\rho+\cdots$ is bounded by the contour method of 21.12.02 fed the zero-free region of 21.13.01; only the principal $\chi_0$ supplies the main term $x/\varphi(q)$ (no pole for non-principal $\chi$ since $L(1,\chi)\neq0$). The **Siegel-Walfisz theorem**: $\psi(x;q,a)=x/\varphi(q)+O(x\exp(-c\sqrt{\log x}))$ uniformly for $q\le(\log x)^A$, with the **exceptional-zero term** $-\overline\chi_1(a)x^\beta/(\beta\varphi(q))$ controlled by **Siegel's ineffective bound** $1-\beta\gg_\varepsilon q^{-\varepsilon}$ (21.13.02); the constant $c(A)$ is therefore ineffective. The **$q\le(\log x)^A$ barrier**: $x^\beta$ beats the main term only while $q^{-\varepsilon}\log x\gg\log q$, forcing a power of $\log x$, not of $x$. The **large sieve / Bombieri-Vinogradov** (21.14.02) recovers $q\le x^{1/2-\varepsilon}$ on average over moduli, unconditionally and effectively, exploiting exceptional-zero rarity. Originators Dirichlet 1837, de la Vallée Poussin 1896, Landau 1918, Siegel 1935, Walfisz 1936, Bombieri-Vinogradov 1965. Lean status none: Mathlib has the character orthogonality and continued $L$-functions but not the twisted explicit formula, the contour bound with exceptional-zero term, the orthogonality assembly, or the Siegel-Walfisz uniformity.

### number-theory.bombieri-vinogradov-theorem

- **title**: The Bombieri-Vinogradov Theorem
- **unit**: `21.14.02`
- **prerequisites**: `21.14.01` (multiplicative large sieve, arithmetic large sieve, Brun-Titchmarsh), `21.13.01` (zero-free regions, Siegel zero, Siegel-Walfisz input), `21.11.04` (truncated Perron formula / Mellin inversion, the explicit-formula bridge)
- **notes**: stub - see unit `21.14.02`. The Bombieri-Vinogradov theorem $\sum_{q\le Q}\max_{(a,q)=1}|\psi(x;q,a)-x/\varphi(q)|\ll_A x(\log x)^{-A}$ for $Q=x^{1/2}(\log x)^{-B}$: primes equidistribute in arithmetic progressions on average over moduli up to $x^{1/2}$, i.e. level of distribution $\vartheta=1/2$, "GRH on average". Proof: character detection $\psi(x;q,a)=\varphi(q)^{-1}\sum_\chi\bar\chi(a)\psi(x,\chi)$, reduction to primitive characters, Siegel-Walfisz for small conductor ($q\le(\log x)^A$, via 21.13.01), Vaughan's identity decomposing $\Lambda$ into Type I / Type II bilinear sums, the multiplicative large sieve (21.14.01) bounding the Type II part after Cauchy-Schwarz, and Perron inversion (21.11.04) back to $\psi(x;q,a)$. Equivalent density-estimate route: $\sum_{q\le Q}(q/\varphi(q))\sum^*_\chi N(\alpha,T,\chi)\ll(Q^2T)^{c(1-\alpha)}$, zeros off the line rare on average. Comparison ladder: Siegel-Walfisz ($q\le\log^A x$, individual) < Bombieri-Vinogradov ($q\le x^{1/2}$, averaged) < GRH ($q\le x^{1/2-\varepsilon}$, individual) < Elliott-Halberstam ($q\le x^{1-\varepsilon}$, averaged, conjectural). The $x^{1/2}$ threshold is forced by the $M+Q^2$ large-sieve constant at length $M\approx x$. Elliott-Halberstam and its role in bounded gaps (GPY, Zhang's smooth-modulus level past 1/2, Maynard's gap 600/12). Parity barrier (21.14.04) blocks reaching the twin gap 2. Bombieri 1965, A. I. Vinogradov 1965, Vaughan 1980; Montgomery-Vaughan §9, Davenport Ch. 28. Lean status none.


### dynamics.measure-preserving-systems-poincare-recurrence-kac

- **title**: Measure-Preserving Systems, Poincaré Recurrence, and the Kac Formula
- **unit**: `38.04.01`
- **prerequisites**: `02.07.01` (sigma-algebra, measurable space, Borel), `02.07.07` (Fubini-Tonelli, product measures), `37.02.03` (Birkhoff/von Neumann/Kingman ergodic theorems)
- **notes**: stub - see unit `38.04.01`. Measure-preserving system $(X,\mathcal{B},\mu,T)$ with $\mu(T^{-1}B)=\mu(B)$; the Koopman operator $U_Tf=f\circ T$ as an $L^2$ isometry (unitary when invertible); canonical examples (irrational rotation, doubling map, Bernoulli shift, Gauss map / continued fractions, toral automorphism). Poincaré recurrence: a.e. point of a positive-measure set returns infinitely often, proved by the no-positive-measure-wandering-set argument. First-return time $r_A$, induced transformation $T_A$, Kakutani skyscraper. Kac formula $\int_A r_A\,d\mu = \mu(\bigcup T^{-n}A)$, equal to $1$ (mean return time $1/\mu(A)$) when ergodic. Kakutani-Rokhlin tower lemma for aperiodic systems. Foundational measure-preserving-dynamics unit (Walters Ch. 1); ergodic-theorem convergence is at 37.02.03. Lean status none: Mathlib lacks the induced map, the Kac formula, the Rokhlin tower, and the named Koopman isometry/spectral apparatus.

### dynamics.dynamical-systems-orbits-limit-sets

- **title**: Dynamical Systems, Orbits, and Limit Sets
- **unit**: `38.01.01`
- **prerequisites**: `02.12.02` (phase flow / one-parameter group), `02.01.05` (metric space), `02.12.01` (phase space, vector field, integral curve)
- **notes**: stub - see unit `38.01.01`. Dynamical system as a group/semigroup action: discrete (a map $f:X\to X$ and its iterates) and continuous (a flow $\varphi_t$). Forward/backward/full orbits; fixed and periodic points; omega- and alpha-limit sets and their structure (closed, compact, invariant, connected for flows / precompact orbits); invariant sets, attractors and basins; the non-wandering set $\Omega(f)$ (closed, invariant, contains periodic points). Topological conjugacy ($h\circ f=g\circ h$) and flow equivalence as the equivalence of systems; conjugacy transports orbits, periodic points, limit sets, $\Omega$, and entropy. Canonical examples: circle rotation (minimal, irrational), doubling map $E_2$ (semiconjugate to the one-sided shift, chaotic), hyperbolic toral automorphism / Arnold cat map ($\#\mathrm{Fix}(f_A^n)=|\det(A^n-I)|$, entropy $\log|\lambda|$), the Smale horseshoe (Cantor invariant set conjugate to the two-sided shift, preview), the logistic map (period-doubling cascade, Feigenbaum universality). The foundational unit of topological dynamics (Brin-Stuck Ch. 1). Distinct from ODE-flow units 02.12.* (abstract topological-dynamics framing). Lean status none: Mathlib has `omegaLimit`, `PeriodicPts`, `Flow` scaffolding but not the limit-set structure package, the non-wandering set, topological conjugacy as a structure, or the shift / canonical-example dynamical APIs.


### dynamics.minimality-recurrence

- **title**: Minimality and Recurrence
- **unit**: `38.01.02`
- **prerequisites**: `38.01.01` (dynamical systems, orbits, limit sets), `02.01.05` (metric space)
- **notes**: stub - see unit `38.01.02`. Minimal sets (no proper non-empty closed invariant subset) and minimal systems (every orbit dense); existence of a minimal set inside any non-empty compact invariant set via Zorn's lemma on closed invariant sets ordered by reverse inclusion. Recurrent ($x\in\omega(x)$), non-wandering, and almost-periodic (uniformly recurrent) points; syndetic ($=$ bounded-gap) return-time sets; the implication chain periodic $\Rightarrow$ almost periodic $\Rightarrow$ recurrent $\Rightarrow$ non-wandering, each strict.
