Birkhoff recurrence theorem: every compact system has a minimal set; $x$ is almost periodic iff $\overline{\mathcal{O}^+(x)}$ is minimal; the system is minimal iff every point is almost periodic. Minimal sets as orbit closures of uniformly recurrent points; two minimal sets equal or disjoint. Topological recurrence (compactness + Zorn, no measure) vs measure-theoretic Poincaré recurrence (38.04.01, needs invariant measure), coinciding on minimal uniquely ergodic systems; Furstenberg multiple recurrence $\to$ van der Waerden. Gottschalk-Hedlund theorem: over a minimal homeomorphism, a continuous $g$ has uniformly bounded Birkhoff sums iff $g=h\circ f-h$ is a continuous coboundary. Equicontinuous minimal systems $\cong$ minimal rotations on compact abelian groups (Halmos-von Neumann); distal structure theorem (Furstenberg 1963). Examples: irrational rotation minimal, doubling map not. Foundational topological-dynamics unit (Brin-Stuck Ch. 1, Gottschalk-Hedlund 1955). Lean status none: Mathlib has `omegaLimit`, `IsMinimal`, `BirkhoffSum` fragments but not the Zorn minimal-set existence theorem, the syndetic almost-periodic predicate, the Birkhoff recurrence equivalence, or the Gottschalk-Hedlund coboundary criterion.

### dynamics.ergodicity-unique-ergodicity-equidistribution
Ergodicity and its equivalent characterisations (invariant sets null/conull, invariant L^2 functions constant, simple Koopman eigenvalue 1, Cesàro correlation decay, Birkhoff limit constant); unique ergodicity as a single invariant measure equivalent to uniform convergence of Birkhoff averages; Weyl equidistribution of (n·alpha) as unique ergodicity of the irrational rotation; ergodic decomposition.


### dynamics.shift-spaces-subshifts

- **title**: Shift Spaces and Subshifts
- **unit**: `38.02.01`
- **prerequisites**: `38.01.01` (dynamical systems, orbits, limit sets, topological conjugacy), `02.01.05` (metric space), `02.01.01` (topological space)
- **notes**: stub - see unit `38.02.01`. The full shift $A^{\mathbb{Z}}$ (or $A^{\mathbb{N}}$) on a finite alphabet with the product/cylinder topology, a compact perfect totally-disconnected Cantor set under the metric $d(x,y)=2^{-\min\{|n|:x_n\neq y_n\}}$; the shift map $\sigma$, continuous, expansive (constant $1$), with $|A|^n$ period-$n$ points, dense periodic points and a dense orbit (Devaney-chaotic). Subshifts = closed shift-invariant subsets, characterised by a set of forbidden words / by the language $\mathcal{L}(X)$; cylinder sets as a clopen basis. Subshifts of finite type and topological Markov chains via $0/1$ transition matrices (the topological analogue of the stochastic matrix in 37.05.02; entropy $=\log\lambda_T$, period-$n$ count $=\operatorname{tr}(T^n)$); sofic shifts as SFT factors / regular languages (even shift). Sliding-block codes and the Curtis-Hedlund-Lyndon theorem: continuous shift-commuting maps are exactly sliding-block codes. Sturmian subshifts from coded irrational rotations (complexity $p(n)=n+1$, Morse-Hedlund). Foundational symbolic-dynamics unit (Brin-Stuck Ch. 3; Lind-Marcus). Lean status none: Mathlib has product topology and the PiNat metric but no packaged shift map, subshift structure, forbidden-word characterisation, or Curtis-Hedlund-Lyndon theorem.

### dynamics.kolmogorov-sinai-entropy-generator-theorem

- **title**: Kolmogorov-Sinai Entropy and the Generator Theorem
- **unit**: `38.06.02`
- **prerequisites**: `38.04.01` (measure-preserving systems, Poincaré recurrence, Kac), `37.02.03` (Birkhoff/von Neumann/Kingman ergodic theorems), `02.07.06` (L^p spaces, completeness)
- **notes**: stub - see unit `38.06.02`. Entropy of a partition H(P)=-sum mu(P_i) log mu(P_i); conditional entropy and the chain rule H(P join Q)=H(Q)+H(P|Q); entropy of a partition relative to T, h(T,P)=lim (1/n) H(join_{k<n} T^{-k}P) (exists by subadditivity/Fekete); Kolmogorov-Sinai entropy h(T)=sup_P h(T,P) as a measurable-conjugacy invariant. Kolmogorov-Sinai generator theorem: h(T)=h(T,P) for any generating partition, collapsing the supremum to one computation (proof via H(Q | P_{-m}^m) -> 0 martingale convergence). Model computations: Bernoulli B(p_1,...,p_k) has entropy -sum p_i log p_i, doubling map log 2, hyperbolic toral automorphism sum_{|lambda_i|>1} log|lambda_i|. Distinguishes Bernoulli shifts (Kolmogorov's solution; Ornstein converse). Shannon-McMillan-Breiman AEP. Measure-theoretic (not physical Boltzmann/Gibbs) entropy; Walters Ch. 4. Lean status none: Mathlib lacks partition entropy, conditional entropy/chain rule, the join structure, h(T,P), h(T), the generator theorem, and the model computations.


### dynamics.hyperbolic-sets-anosov-axiom-a-smale-decomposition

- **title**: Hyperbolic Sets, Anosov and Axiom-A Systems, and the Smale Spectral Decomposition
- **unit**: `38.03.01`
- **prerequisites**: `38.01.01` (dynamical systems, orbits, limit sets, non-wandering set, conjugacy), `02.12.02` (phase flow / one-parameter group), `02.12.08` (Lyapunov stability, direct method)
- **notes**: stub - see unit `38.03.01`. Hyperbolic set: compact $f$-invariant $\Lambda$ with continuous $Df$-invariant splitting $T_\Lambda M = E^s \oplus E^u$, uniform contraction on $E^s$ / expansion on $E^u$ ($\|Df^n v\|\le C\lambda^n\|v\|$, $0<\lambda<1$). Adapted (Lyapunov) metric gives $C=1$; cone criterion makes hyperbolicity $C^1$-open hence robust; splitting unique, continuous, generically only Hölder. Anosov diffeomorphism ($\Lambda=M$; model = $\mathrm{SL}(n,\mathbb{Z})$ hyperbolic toral automorphism / cat map) and Anosov flow ($TM=E^s\oplus E^0\oplus E^u$; geodesic flow on a negatively curved manifold, Anosov 1967). Axiom A: $\Omega(f)$ hyperbolic + periodic points dense in $\Omega(f)$. Smale spectral decomposition: $\Omega(f)=\Omega_1\sqcup\cdots\sqcup\Omega_k$ finite union of disjoint closed topologically transitive basic sets, each cyclically partitioned into topologically mixing components; proved via the equivalence relation $p\sim q$ ($W^u(p)\pitchfork W^s(q)$ both ways), the inclination ($\lambda$-)lemma, local product structure, and the Anosov closing/shadowing lemma. Expansiveness, stable/unstable manifold theorem (graph transform), structural stability of Anosov maps, $\Omega$-stability + no-cycle condition, Markov partitions / subshift-of-finite-type coding (Bowen), entropy $=\log\lambda$. Root unit of hyperbolic dynamics (Katok-Hasselblatt Ch. 6, 17-18; Smale 1967). Lean status none: Mathlib has TangentBundle/mfderiv but no HyperbolicSet predicate, cone field, stable-manifold theorem, Anosov/Axiom-A classes, or spectral decomposition.


### dynamics.oseledets-multiplicative-ergodic-theorem-lyapunov-exponents

- **title**: The Oseledets Multiplicative Ergodic Theorem and Lyapunov Exponents
- **unit**: `38.07.01`
- **prerequisites**: `38.04.01` (measure-preserving systems, Poincaré recurrence, Kac), `37.02.03` (Birkhoff/von Neumann/Kingman ergodic theorems), `02.12.08` (Lyapunov stability, direct method)
- **notes**: stub - see unit `38.07.01`. Lyapunov exponents as asymptotic exponential growth rates $\lambda(x,v)=\limsup\frac1n\log\|A^{(n)}(x)v\|$ of tangent vectors under a measurable linear cocycle $A:X\to\mathrm{GL}(d,\mathbb{R})$ with cocycle identity $A^{(n+m)}(x)=A^{(m)}(T^nx)A^{(n)}(x)$, over a measure-preserving system with $\log^+\|A^{\pm1}\|$ integrable. Furstenberg-Kesten: the top exponent $\lambda_1=\lim\frac1n\log\|A^{(n)}\|=\inf_n\frac1n\int\log\|A^{(n)}\|$ exists a.e. via Kingman (37.02.03) applied to the submultiplicative $\log\|\wedge^kA^{(n)}\|=\log(\sigma_1\cdots\sigma_k)$. Oseledets MET: a.e. $x$ has filtration $V_1\supsetneq\cdots\supsetneq V_r$ with distinct exponents $\lambda_1>\cdots>\lambda_r$ and multiplicities $d_i$, $\frac1n\log\|A^{(n)}v\|\to\lambda_i$ on $V_i\setminus V_{i+1}$; the two-sided/invertible version gives the equivariant Oseledets splitting $\mathbb{R}^d=\bigoplus E_i(x)$, $A(x)E_i(x)=E_i(Tx)$, with genuine two-sided limits, tempered (subexponential) angles, and the trace identity $\sum d_i\lambda_i=\int\log|\det A|\,d\mu$. Limiting matrix $(A^{(n)*}A^{(n)})^{1/2n}\to\Lambda(x)$ with eigenvalues $e^{\lambda_i}$; Raghunathan's exterior-power proof. The gateway to nonuniform hyperbolicity / Pesin theory (derivative cocycle $A(x)=D_xf$, stable-manifold theorem, Pesin-Ruelle entropy inequality and formula). Oseledets 1968; Furstenberg-Kesten 1960; Katok-Hasselblatt Suppl. Lean status none: Mathlib lacks measurable linear cocycles, the subadditive (non-additive) Kingman theorem, and the Oseledets splitting/filtration.


### dynamics.topological-transitivity-mixing-devaney-chaos

- **title**: Topological Transitivity, Topological Mixing, and Devaney Chaos
- **unit**: `38.01.03`
- **prerequisites**: `38.01.01` (dynamical systems, orbits, limit sets, non-wandering set), `38.01.02` (minimality and recurrence)
- **notes**: stub - see unit `38.01.03`. Topological transitivity: for every pair of non-empty open $U,V$ there is $n$ with $f^n(U)\cap V\neq\varnothing$; Birkhoff transitivity theorem — on a perfect (no isolated points) compact metric / second-countable Baire space this is equivalent to the existence of a dense forward orbit, and the dense-orbit points form a dense $G_\delta$ (proof = Baire-category intersection of the dense open sets $W_j=\bigcup_n f^{-n}(V_j)$). Topological mixing: the return set $N(U,V)=\{n:f^n(U)\cap V\neq\varnothing\}$ is cofinite for every pair. Strict hierarchy minimal $\Rightarrow$ transitive $\Leftarrow$ mixing, the two strengthenings incomparable (irrational rotation minimal+transitive but not mixing/chaotic; doubling map and full shift mixing+transitive+chaotic but not minimal). Sensitive dependence on initial conditions (uniform constant $\delta$). Devaney chaos = transitivity + dense periodic points + sensitivity; Banks-Brooks-Cairns-Davis-Stacey 1992 theorem that transitivity + dense periodic points $\Rightarrow$ sensitivity (so (D3) is redundant on any metric space with $>1$ point). Canonical chaotic models: doubling map $E_2$ and full one-sided shift $\sigma$ on $\{0,1\}^{\mathbb N}$ (dyadic interval / cylinder maps onto everything after finitely many steps). Topological shadow of the measurable ergodic/weakly-mixing/strongly-mixing spectral hierarchy. Brin-Stuck Ch. 1; Devaney Ch. 1. Lean status none: Mathlib has `Baire`, `Minimal`, `OmegaLimit`, `PeriodicPts` but no `TopologicallyTransitive`/`TopologicallyMixing`/`SensitiveDependence`/`DevaneyChaos` predicates, no Birkhoff transitivity theorem, no Banks et al. redundancy theorem, and no transitivity/mixing API for the doubling map or full shift.


### dynamics.circle-rotations-unique-ergodicity

- **title**: Circle Rotations and Unique Ergodicity
- **unit**: `38.01.04`
- **prerequisites**: `38.01.02` (minimality and recurrence), `38.04.02` (ergodicity, unique ergodicity, equidistribution), `21.15.02` (Weyl sums, Weyl differencing, equidistribution)
- **notes**: stub - see unit `38.01.04`. The circle rotation $R_\alpha(x)=x+\alpha\bmod 1$ and the rational/irrational dichotomy: rational $\alpha=p/q$ gives $R_\alpha^q=\mathrm{id}$ with every orbit periodic of period $q$ (not minimal, not uniquely ergodic); irrational $\alpha$ gives a minimal system (every orbit dense) that is uniquely ergodic with Lebesgue the unique invariant measure. Minimality proved by the pigeonhole / three-distance return $\{m\alpha\}$ near $0$. Unique ergodicity by pinning Fourier coefficients $\hat\nu(k)(e^{2\pi i k\alpha}-1)=0$, yielding Weyl equidistribution of $(n\alpha)$ via the Oxtoby uniform-convergence theorem - the dynamical proof complementing the Fourier-analytic Weyl criterion (21.15.02). Continued-fraction convergents $p_k/q_k$ as the record-close returns ($|q_k\alpha-p_k|<1/q_{k+1}$) and the Steinhaus three-distance theorem on orbit gap structure. Rotations as the model equicontinuous/isometric systems (zero topological entropy) and the Halmos-von Neumann discrete-spectrum representatives (eigenvalues $e^{2\pi i k\alpha}$ a complete isomorphism invariant). Brin-Stuck Ch. 4. Lean status none: Mathlib has AddCircle and the rotation but lacks the minimality dichotomy, the pigeonhole/three-distance argument, unique ergodicity as a predicate, the Oxtoby/Weyl equidistribution chain, and the continued-fraction / Halmos-von Neumann structure.


### dynamics.shifts-finite-type-transition-matrices-coding

- **title**: Shifts of Finite Type, Transition Matrices, and Coding
- **unit**: `38.02.02`
- **prerequisites**: `38.02.01` (shift spaces, subshifts, forbidden words, sliding-block codes, Curtis-Hedlund-Lyndon)
- **notes**: stub - see unit `38.02.02`. Subshifts of finite type (SFTs) / topological Markov chains: the vertex/edge shift cut out by a $0/1$ transition (adjacency) matrix $A$, allowed sequences = bi-infinite walks in the support graph $G_A$ ($\mathsf{X}_A=\{x:A_{x_n x_{n+1}}=1\}$). Dictionary: $A$ irreducible (graph strongly connected) iff topologically transitive; $A$ primitive (strongly connected + aperiodic) iff topologically mixing; period $d(A)=\gcd$ of closed-walk lengths. Periodic-point count $\#\mathrm{Fix}(\sigma^n)=\operatorname{tr}(A^n)$ and rational Artin-Mazur zeta function $\zeta_\sigma(t)=\exp\sum \operatorname{tr}(A^n)t^n/n=\det(I-tA)^{-1}$ (Bowen-Lanford). Higher-block presentation: every SFT is conjugate to a $1$-step / edge SFT. Conjugacy of edge shifts = strong shift equivalence of non-negative integer matrices, generated by state splitting (Williams Decomposition Theorem; Williams' conjecture false, Kim-Roush). Entropy $=\log\lambda_A$, Perron eigenvalue; Lind: SFT growth rates are exactly the Perron numbers. Sofic shifts as sliding-block factors of SFTs / regular languages (Fischer, Krieger covers; even shift). The $0/1$ matrix is the topological analogue of the stochastic matrix in 37.05.02 (distinct object, same support graph). Brin-Stuck Ch. 3; Lind-Marcus. Lean status none: Mathlib has matrix trace/charpoly and Perron-Frobenius but no edge/vertex shift, transition-matrix SFT, walk-count periodic-point theorem, higher-block conjugacy, or strong shift equivalence.


### dynamics.shannon-mcmillan-breiman-theorem

- **title**: The Shannon-McMillan-Breiman Theorem
- **unit**: `38.06.03`
- **prerequisites**: `38.06.02` (Kolmogorov-Sinai entropy and the generator theorem), `37.02.03` (Birkhoff/von Neumann/Kingman ergodic theorems)
- **notes**: stub - see unit `38.06.03`. The Shannon-McMillan-Breiman theorem (the asymptotic equipartition property): for an ergodic measure-preserving system and a finite partition P with H(P)<infinity, the normalised information -(1/n) log mu(P^n(x)) -> h(T,P) for a.e. x and in L^1, where P^n(x) is the atom of join_{k<n} T^{-k}P containing x; for a generating P the limit is h(T). Proof via the information cocycle (chain-rule decomposition I_{P^n}=sum_j g_{n-1-j} o T^j), increasing-martingale convergence of the conditional informations g_m -> f=I_P(.|P_infty), a maximal inequality bounding sup_m g_m in L^1, and Birkhoff applied to f. Typical-set / AEP interpretation: about e^{nh} nearly-equiprobable length-n names of measure e^{-nh} carry almost all the mass; Shannon source-coding floor h nats per symbol. Brin-Katok local-entropy formula h_mu(T)=lim_{eps->0} limsup_n -(1/n) log mu(B(x,n,eps)) (statement); Ornstein-Weiss return times. Shannon 1948 (in probability), McMillan 1953 (L^1), Breiman 1957 (a.e.); Walters Ch. 4. Lean status none: Mathlib lacks partition/conditional/information-function entropy, h(T,P), the SMB theorem and its cocycle/martingale/maximal-inequality proof, the AEP and typical sets, and the Brin-Katok formula.


### dynamics.mixing-hierarchy-weak-mixing-ergodicity

- **title**: The Mixing Hierarchy: Mixing, Weak Mixing, and Ergodicity
- **unit**: `38.05.01`
- **prerequisites**: `38.04.02` (ergodicity, unique ergodicity, equidistribution), `37.02.03` (Birkhoff/von Neumann/Kingman ergodic theorems), `02.11.08` (Hilbert space)
- **notes**: stub - see unit `38.05.01`. The mixing hierarchy strong mixing => weak mixing => ergodic, with the measure-theoretic correlation definitions: strong mixing mu(T^-n A cap B) -> mu(A)mu(B); weak mixing (1/n) sum |mu(T^-k A cap B) - mu(A)mu(B)| -> 0; ergodic the signed Cesaro average. Equivalent characterisations of weak mixing (the Koopman-von Neumann theorem): no nonconstant L^2 eigenfunctions of U_T (continuous spectrum on L^2_0, the orthocomplement of constants); T x T ergodic; density-one decay of correlations. The Koopman-von Neumann lemma: bounded nonnegative a_k has Cesaro mean -> 0 iff a_k -> 0 along a density-one set iff the Cesaro mean of a_k^2 -> 0; proof via Herglotz-Bochner spectral measure sigma_f, atoms = eigenfunctions, weak mixing = non-atomic sigma_f. Strict separations: irrational rotation ergodic-not-weak-mixing (eigenfunction e^(2 pi i x)); Chacon / rank-one rigid weak-but-not-strong-mixing. Mixing examples: Bernoulli shifts, hyperbolic toral automorphisms / cat map (character escape U_T chi_m = chi_(A^T m)). Spectral ladder point/continuous/Rajchman; genericity (weak mixing residual, mixing meagre; Rokhlin/Halmos). Preview of spectral theory 38.05.02. Walters Ch. 1-2. Lean status none: Mathlib lacks StronglyMixing/WeaklyMixing predicates, the implication chain and its strictness, the Koopman-von Neumann lemma and its spectral consequences, and the toral/Bernoulli mixing computations.


### dynamics.hadamard-perron-stable-unstable-manifold-theorem

- **title**: The Hadamard-Perron Stable and Unstable Manifold Theorem
- **unit**: `38.03.03`
- **prerequisites**: `38.03.01` (hyperbolic sets, Anosov, Axiom A, Smale spectral decomposition), `38.01.01` (dynamical systems, orbits, limit sets, non-wandering set, conjugacy), `02.12.08` (Lyapunov stability, direct method)
- **notes**: stub - see unit `38.03.03`. Hadamard-Perron stable/unstable manifold theorem at a point $x$ of a hyperbolic set: the local stable set $W^s_\varepsilon(x)=\{y:d(f^n x,f^n y)\le\varepsilon\ \forall n\ge0\}$ is a $C^r$ embedded disc tangent to $E^s_x$ (graph of $\sigma_x$, $\sigma_x(0)=0$, $D\sigma_x(0)=0$) with $d(f^n x,f^n y)\le C\lambda^n d(x,y)$; symmetric $W^u$ tangent to $E^u$. Two proofs: the Hadamard graph-transform method (contraction $\Gamma$ on Lipschitz sections of the stable cone bundle, $\|\Gamma\sigma-\Gamma\tau\|\le(\lambda+2\eta)\|\sigma-\tau\|$) and the Perron method (fixed point of a variation-of-constants summation operator on decaying sequences, stable part solved forward / unstable backward); the two manifolds coincide as the set of initial conditions of decaying orbits. $C^r$ regularity via the fiber-contraction theorem / $C^r$ section theorem (Hirsch-Pugh-Shub); continuous (Holder) dependence on $x$. Global $W^s(x)=\bigcup_n f^{-n}W^s_\varepsilon(f^n x)$ an injectively immersed copy of $E^s_x$. Inclination/$\lambda$-lemma: discs transverse to $W^s_{loc}$ converge $C^1$ to $W^u_{loc}$ (slope contracts by $\lambda^2$); local product structure $W^s_\varepsilon(x)\cap W^u_\varepsilon(y)=\{[x,y]\}$; absolute continuity of the stable foliation. Underlies the Smale spectral decomposition's transversality relation and structural stability (Katok-Hasselblatt Ch. 6 6.2-6.4; Hadamard 1901; Perron 1928). Lean status none: Mathlib has ContractingWith/fderiv/TangentBundle but no graph transform, section space, fiber-contraction/section theorem, Perron operator, or lambda-lemma.


### dynamics.pesin-theory-entropy-formula

- **title**: Pesin Theory and the Entropy Formula
- **unit**: `38.07.02`
- **prerequisites**: `38.07.01` (Oseledets MET, Lyapunov exponents, Oseledets splitting), `38.06.02` (Kolmogorov-Sinai entropy, generator theorem), `38.03.01` (hyperbolic sets, Anosov/Axiom A, stable-manifold theorem)
- **notes**: stub - see unit `38.07.02`. Nonuniform hyperbolicity: a measure with all Lyapunov exponents nonzero a.e. (37.02.03/38.07.01) splits the tangent space $T_xM=E^s(x)\oplus E^u(x)$ via the Oseledets splitting. Tempering lemma converts subexponential Oseledets bounds into slowly-varying ($\varepsilon$-tempered) constants; Pesin sets / Lyapunov-regular blocks $\Lambda_\ell$ carry uniform hyperbolicity with constant $\ell$, are compact, increasing, full-measure, but non-invariant ($f(\Lambda_\ell)\subseteq\Lambda_{e^\varepsilon\ell}$). Nonuniform Hadamard-Perron / Pesin stable-manifold theorem: a.e. point has $C^{1+\alpha}$ local stable/unstable manifolds $W^{s/u}_{loc}(x)$ tangent to $E^{s/u}(x)$, radius $r(x)$ tempered, Holder-continuous within a block. Margulis-Ruelle inequality $h_\mu(f)\le\int\sum_i\lambda_i^+\,d\mu$ (entropy $\le$ unstable volume growth $\int\log|\det Df|_{E^u}|$) with proof via partition refinement and conditional-entropy counting. Pesin's entropy formula $h_\mu(f)=\int\sum_i\lambda_i^+\,d\mu$ for measures absolutely continuous w.r.t. volume (SRB measures); role of absolute continuity of the unstable foliation/lamination. SRB measures: conditionals on $W^u$ absolutely continuous, basin/physical-measure property; existence/uniqueness for Axiom-A attractors (Sinai-Ruelle-Bowen). Ledrappier-Young formula $h_\mu=\sum_i\gamma_i\lambda_i$ (dimension-weighted positive exponents; SRB iff $\gamma_i=d_i$). Pesin 1976/1977; Ruelle 1978; Ledrappier-Young 1985; Katok-Hasselblatt Suppl. Lean status none: Mathlib lacks Pesin sets, tempering, the nonuniform stable-manifold theorem, absolute continuity of the lamination, Margulis-Ruelle, the entropy formula, and Ledrappier-Young.


### dynamics.spectral-theory-halmos-von-neumann

- **title**: Spectral Theory of Dynamical Systems and the Halmos-von Neumann Theorem
- **unit**: `38.05.02`
- **prerequisites**: `38.05.01` (mixing hierarchy, weak mixing, ergodicity), `38.04.02` (ergodicity, unique ergodicity, equidistribution), `02.11.08` (Hilbert space)
- **notes**: stub - see unit `38.05.02`. The spectral viewpoint: a measure-preserving invertible T studied via its Koopman operator U_T f = f o T, a unitary on L^2(mu). Spectral isomorphism = unitary equivalence of Koopman operators (W U_T = U_S W); measurable isomorphism induces it (and preserves constants/L^infty) but is strictly stronger in general. Maximal spectral type and the point/continuous decomposition L^2 = H_pp + H_c (Koopman-von Neumann); discrete (pure point) spectrum = eigenfunctions span L^2. Eigenvalue set e(T) is a subgroup of the circle; under ergodicity eigenvalues are simple and eigenfunctions have constant modulus, so unit-modulus eigenfunctions form a group. Halmos-von Neumann theorem: an ergodic discrete-spectrum system is measurably isomorphic to a rotation x|->x+a on the compact abelian group G = dual(e(T)) (Pontryagin, e(T) discrete) with Haar measure, with eigenfunctions = characters; the eigenvalue group is a complete invariant, so on the discrete-spectrum class spectral isomorphism = measurable isomorphism. Kronecker factor = maximal discrete-spectrum factor (closed span of eigenfunctions); weak mixing iff trivial Kronecker factor. Contrast with weak mixing (continuous spectrum on L^2_0, 38.05.01). Spectral invariants do NOT determine entropy: spectrally isomorphic Bernoulli shifts of different entropy (countable Lebesgue spectrum; Ornstein), Anzai skew product (mixed spectrum) as caveat. Hahn-Hellinger maximal-type/multiplicity invariants; Banach simple-Lebesgue-spectrum problem. Halmos-von Neumann 1942; Koopman 1931; Anzai 1951; Walters Ch. 2-3. Lean status none: Mathlib lacks the Koopman-operator API, spectral-isomorphism predicate, eigenvalue group, the maximal spectral type/point-continuous decomposition, the Halmos-von Neumann classification, the Kronecker factor, and the entropy obstruction.


### dynamics.perron-frobenius-sft-growth-subshift-entropy

- **title**: Perron-Frobenius Theory, SFT Growth Rate, and Subshift Entropy
- **unit**: `38.02.03`
- **prerequisites**: `38.02.02` (SFTs, transition matrices, edge/vertex shifts, periodic-point count tr(A^n), zeta function), `38.01.03` (topological transitivity, topological mixing, Devaney chaos)
- **notes**: stub - see unit `38.02.03`. Perron-Frobenius theorem for non-negative matrices: an irreducible non-negative $A$ has a simple positive Perron root $\lambda=\rho(A)$ (the spectral radius) with strictly positive left/right eigenvectors; $\lambda$ is the unique eigenvalue of maximum modulus iff $A$ is primitive (aperiodic), with $d=\gcd$-of-cycle-lengths peripheral eigenvalues $\lambda e^{2\pi ik/d}$ otherwise. Proofs: Brouwer/cone fixed point + irreducibility for existence/positivity/simplicity; Birkhoff-Hilbert projective-metric contraction (ratio $\tanh(\Delta/4)<1$) for uniqueness and the spectral gap. Application to SFTs: $\#\mathcal{L}_n(\mathsf{X}_A)=\mathbf{1}^\top A^{n-1}\mathbf{1}\sim c\lambda^n$, so topological entropy $h(\mathsf{X}_A)=\log\lambda(A)$ = log Perron root = growth rate of periodic points $\#\mathrm{Fix}(\sigma^n)=\operatorname{tr}(A^n)\sim\lambda^n$; zeta $\zeta(t)=\det(I-tA)^{-1}$ with smallest pole $1/\lambda$. Golden-mean entropy $=\log\varphi$. Lind: SFT growth rates = exactly the Perron numbers (algebraic integer strictly dominating its conjugates). Variational principle: $\log\lambda=\sup_\mu h_\mu$, attained by the Parry measure $P_{ij}=A_{ij}v_j/(\lambda v_i)$, $\pi_i=w_iv_i$ — the diagonal Perron rescaling turning the topological matrix (root $\lambda$) into the stochastic matrix (root $1$) of 37.05.02. Topological counterpart of the stochastic Perron-Frobenius. Brin-Stuck Ch. 3; Lind-Marcus Ch. 4; Seneta; Walters. Lean status none: Mathlib has matrix Perron-Frobenius and some topological-entropy scaffolding but not the entropy = log spectral-radius identification, the primitivity ⇔ unique-dominant-modulus equivalence, the Birkhoff-Hilbert cone-contraction proof, or the Perron-number characterisation.


### dynamics.poincare-rotation-number-denjoy-theorem

- **title**: The Poincaré Rotation Number and Denjoy's Theorem
- **unit**: `38.01.05`
- **prerequisites**: `38.01.04` (circle rotations and unique ergodicity), `38.01.02` (minimality and recurrence)
- **notes**: stub - see unit `38.01.05`. For an orientation-preserving circle homeomorphism $f$ with lift $F$, the Poincaré rotation number $\rho(f)=\lim_n (F^n(x)-x)/n$ exists (subadditivity/Fekete on the displacement $F-\mathrm{id}$), is independent of the basepoint, descends mod $1$, and is a topological-conjugacy invariant; it is continuous and monotone in $f$. Rational $\rho=p/q$ iff $f$ has a periodic orbit (period exactly $q$), proved via the intermediate value theorem on $F^q-\mathrm{id}-p$. Poincaré's classification of irrational $\rho$: the orbit is rigidly rotation-ordered (an orientation-preserving map cannot reorder its orbit), giving a monotone degree-one semiconjugacy $h\circ f=R_\rho\circ h$; either $f$ is minimal and $h$ is a conjugacy, or $f$ has a wandering interval and a unique Cantor minimal set with $h$ collapsing the gaps. Denjoy's theorem: a $C^2$ (more sharply $C^{1+\mathrm{bv}}$, $\log Df$ of bounded variation) diffeomorphism with irrational $\rho$ has no wandering interval, hence is conjugate to $R_\rho$ and minimal; proof by the bounded-distortion estimate $|\log Df^n(x)-\log Df^n(y)|\le\mathrm{Var}(\log Df)$ over disjoint iterates, applied along continued-fraction closest-return blocks. The Denjoy counterexample: a $C^1$ (not $C^{1+\mathrm{bv}}$) diffeomorphism blowing up an orbit into summable-length inserted intervals, with a wandering interval and Cantor minimal set, semiconjugate but not conjugate to $R_\rho$ — showing the smoothness threshold is sharp. Continuity/monotonicity of $\rho$, devil's-staircase / mode-locking and Arnold tongues at hyperbolic rational orbits; Herman-Yoccoz smooth linearisation for Diophantine $\rho$ (preview). Brin-Stuck Ch. 4; Katok-Hasselblatt Ch. 11-12; Poincaré 1885; Denjoy 1932. Lean status none: Mathlib has AddCircle and the rotation but lacks the rotation-number definition and its existence/invariance, the rational-iff-periodic equivalence, Poincaré's semiconjugacy classification, the Denjoy bounded-distortion theorem and counterexample, and the mode-locking/Arnold-tongue structure.


### dynamics.shadowing-structural-stability

- **title**: Shadowing and Structural Stability
- **unit**: `38.03.04`
- **prerequisites**: `38.03.01` (hyperbolic sets, Anosov, Axiom A, Smale spectral decomposition), `38.03.03` (Hadamard-Perron stable/unstable manifold theorem)
- **notes**: stub - see unit `38.03.04`. Pseudo-orbits ($\delta$-chains, $d(f(x_n),x_{n+1})\le\delta$) and $\varepsilon$-shadowing ($d(f^n y,x_n)\le\varepsilon$). Anosov shadowing lemma: near a locally maximal hyperbolic set every $\delta$-pseudo-orbit is $\varepsilon$-shadowed by a true orbit, uniquely on the set; proof as a Banach/Newton fixed point of the orbit-correction operator $L=S-\mathcal{A}$, invertible because the contracting $E^s$ block is inverted forward and expanding $E^u$ block backward ($\|L^{-1}\|\le1/(1-\lambda)$), the discrete Perron summation. Linear shadowing constant $\varepsilon\le L\delta$. Anosov closing lemma as the periodic specialisation (near-return pseudo-orbit shadowed by a genuine periodic orbit), giving density of periodic points. Expansivity + uniqueness of the shadow. Structural stability: a $C^1$-small perturbation $g$ of an Anosov (or Axiom-A + strong transversality) system is topologically conjugate to $f$ via the shadowing map $h(x)=$ basepoint of the $f$-orbit shadowing the $g$-orbit of $x$; $h$ Holder (moduli of stability). Expansive+shadowing $\Rightarrow$ topologically stable (Walters). $\Omega$-stability under the no-cycle condition. Structural-stability theorem (Robbin 1971 $C^2$ / Robinson 1976 $C^1$ sufficiency via shadowing; Mane 1988 $C^1$ necessity = $C^1$ stability conjecture): Axiom A + strong transversality $\iff$ $C^1$-structural stability. Katok-Hasselblatt Ch. 18. Lean status none: Mathlib lacks PseudoOrbit/shadowing predicates, the Anosov shadowing theorem and its fixed-point proof, the closing lemma, topological conjugacy/structural stability, and the Robbin-Robinson-Mane theorem.

### dynamics.smale-horseshoe-homoclinic-theorem

- **title**: The Smale Horseshoe and the Smale-Birkhoff Homoclinic Theorem
- **unit**: `38.03.02`
- **prerequisites**: `38.03.01` (hyperbolic sets, Anosov/Axiom A, stable-manifold theorem, spectral decomposition), `38.02.02` (shifts of finite type, transition matrices, the full 2-shift, coding), `38.01.01` (dynamical systems, orbits, limit sets, non-wandering set)
- **notes**: stub - see unit `38.03.02`. The Smale horseshoe: a diffeomorphism that stretches a square horizontally ($\mu>2$), contracts it vertically ($\lambda<1/2$), folds and lays it back across two vertical strips $V_0,V_1$. Invariant set $\Lambda=\bigcap_{n\in\mathbb{Z}}f^n(S)=C\times C'$ a product of two Cantor sets; itinerary/coding map $h(x)_n=i\iff f^n(x)\in V_i$ is a homeomorphism conjugating $f|_\Lambda$ to the full two-sided 2-shift $\sigma$ (transition matrix all-ones, nothing forbidden). Hence hyperbolic basic set with $\#\mathrm{Fix}(f^n)=2^n$, dense periodic points, dense orbit, sensitive dependence, topological entropy $\log 2$, all structurally stable (Conley-Moser conditions open + $C^1$-openness of hyperbolicity). Transverse homoclinic point $q\in W^s(p)\pitchfork W^u(p)$; the homoclinic tangle (Poincaré, three-body problem); invariance forces infinitely many transverse intersections accumulating on $p$. Smale-Birkhoff homoclinic theorem: a transverse homoclinic point forces a horseshoe (full shift) in some iterate $g^N$, hence positive entropy and chaos. The $\lambda$-lemma / inclination lemma (Palis): forward iterates of a disc transverse to $W^s(p)$ converge $C^1$ to $W^u(p)$ — the engine. Melnikov's method (statement): $M(t_0)=\int X_0(z_0(t))\wedge X_1(z_0(t),t+t_0)\,dt$; a simple zero gives transverse homoclinic points in a periodically forced system (Duffing/pendulum), hence via Smale-Birkhoff a horseshoe. Smale 1965/1967; Birkhoff 1935; Palis 1969; Moser 1973; Melnikov 1963; Guckenheimer-Holmes 1983; Katok-Hasselblatt Ch. 2 §2.5, Ch. 6 §6.5-6.6. Lean status none: Mathlib lacks the horseshoe diffeomorphism, the Conley-Moser conditions, the itinerary conjugacy to the full shift, the $\lambda$-lemma (needs the stable-manifold theorem), the Smale-Birkhoff theorem, and any Melnikov/perturbative ODE layer.

### dynamics.topological-pressure-variational-principle-equilibrium-states

- **title**: Topological Pressure, the Variational Principle, and Equilibrium States
- **unit**: `38.06.04`
- **prerequisites**: `38.06.02` (Kolmogorov-Sinai entropy, generator theorem), `38.06.03` (Shannon-McMillan-Breiman theorem)
- **notes**: stub - see unit `38.06.04`. Topological pressure P(f,phi) of a continuous potential phi via weighted (n,epsilon)-separated/spanning sets, Z_n=sup sum_E exp(S_n phi), and the equivalent open-cover definition; topological entropy h_top(f)=P(f,0) the phi=0 case. Variational principle P(f,phi)=sup_mu (h_mu(f)+integral phi dmu) over invariant measures (Ruelle 1973, Walters 1975), with full proof of both inequalities (lower bound via Gibbs inequality + partition estimate, upper bound via empirical measures from near-maximal separated sets / Misiurewicz). Entropy variational principle h_top=sup_mu h_mu the phi=0 shadow. Equilibrium states (measures attaining the sup); existence via upper semicontinuity of entropy on expansive systems; equilibrium states = tangent functionals to the convex pressure functional (Legendre duality, uniqueness = differentiability of P). Measure of maximal entropy (phi=0 equilibrium state); Parry measure on an irreducible SFT from Perron-Frobenius eigenvectors, h_top=log lambda_A. Uniqueness for Holder potentials on transitive Anosov/Axiom-A via Ruelle-Perron-Frobenius transfer operator; Gibbs property mu(C_n)~exp(-nP+S_n phi). Thermodynamic dictionary: pressure<->free energy, equilibrium<->Gibbs state, phase transition<->non-unique equilibrium state; SRB measures as equilibrium states of phi^u=-log|det Df|E^u| with P=0 (Ruelle inequality, Pesin formula equality). Walters Ch. 9; Bowen LNM 470; Ruelle thermodynamic formalism. Lean status none: Mathlib lacks topological entropy/pressure, the variational principle, equilibrium states, the Parry measure, the transfer operator, and the SRB identification.

### dynamics.hopf-argument-ergodicity-geodesic-anosov-flows

- **title**: The Hopf Argument for Ergodicity of Geodesic and Anosov Flows
- **unit**: `38.07.03`
- **prerequisites**: `38.03.01` (hyperbolic sets, Anosov/Axiom A, stable and unstable manifolds), `38.04.02` (ergodicity, unique ergodicity, equidistribution), `38.05.01` (mixing hierarchy: mixing, weak mixing, ergodicity)
- **notes**: stub - see unit `38.07.03`. The Hopf argument for ergodicity of a volume-preserving Anosov flow/diffeomorphism: the forward Birkhoff average $f^+$ of a continuous $f$ is constant along stable manifolds $W^s$ (merging forward orbits + uniform continuity + Cesaro), the backward average $f^-$ is constant along unstable manifolds $W^u$, and $f^+=f^-$ a.e. by Birkhoff. Local product structure $W^s_{loc}\times W^u_{loc}\times$(flow) plus ABSOLUTE CONTINUITY of the stable/unstable foliations (Anosov-Sinai: holonomy between transversals nonsingular with bounded Jacobian, the convergent infinite-product formula) license a Fubini step promoting the two leafwise constancies to local constancy; transitivity + connectedness give global constancy, hence ergodicity (38.04.02). The foliations are only Holder (not smooth), so naive Fubini fails and absolute continuity is the analytic crux. Applications: geodesic flow on a compact negatively curved manifold is Anosov (Jacobi-field/Riccati instability), ergodic, mixing, and Bernoulli (Anosov 1967; Markov partitions Bowen-Ratner + Ornstein-Weiss); volume-preserving transitive Anosov diffeomorphisms / the cat map (linear = affine-holonomy foliations, so smooth Fubini, elementary). Hopf's original constant-negative-curvature case (1939) used smooth horocycle foliations. Abstract Hopf criterion: two transverse partitions on which time averages are measurable, joined mod m. Katok-Hasselblatt Ch. 17; Hopf 1939; Anosov 1967; Anosov-Sinai 1967. Lean status none: Mathlib lacks AnosovFlow, the stable-manifold theorem, the leafwise-constancy lemma, local product structure, and absolute continuity of the foliations.


### dynamics.topological-entropy

- **title**: Topological Entropy
- **unit**: `38.06.01`
- **prerequisites**: `38.01.03` (topological transitivity, mixing, Devaney chaos), `38.02.02` (shifts of finite type, transition matrices, coding)
- **notes**: stub - see unit `38.06.01`. Topological entropy h_top(f) of a continuous map of a compact metric space. Adler-Konheim-McAndrew open-cover definition: h(f,U)=lim (1/n) log N(join_{k<n} f^{-k}U) (exists by subadditivity log N(U vee V) <= log N(U)+log N(V) / Fekete), h_top(f)=sup_U h(f,U). Equivalent Bowen-Dinaburg metric definition via the Bowen metric d_n(x,y)=max_{k<n} d(f^k x,f^k y), (n,eps)-separated sets s(n,eps) and (n,eps)-spanning sets r(n,eps) with r(n,eps)<=s(n,eps)<=r(n,eps/2); h=lim_{eps->0} limsup_n (1/n) log s(n,eps) = open-cover entropy (proof via Lebesgue number relating cover atoms to Bowen balls). Properties: conjugacy invariance, h(f^m)=m h(f), h(f^{-1})=h(f), h(fxg)=h(f)+h(g), factor inequality h(g)<=h(f). Model computations: full shift on k symbols log k; SFT X_A log lambda(A) (Perron eigenvalue, W_n=1^T A^{n-1} 1, cross-ref 38.02.02 Perron-Frobenius); expanding map x->mx mod 1 has log m=log(degree); golden-mean shift log((1+sqrt5)/2); hyperbolic toral automorphism sum_{|lambda_i|>1} log|lambda_i| (Bowen-ball-as-rectangle count, constant-exponent Pesin prototype). Volume-growth identity: Yomdin (1987) / Newhouse (1988-89) h_top of a C^infty map = exponential growth rate of vol(f^n . sigma) over C^infty cubes. Connects to the variational principle h_top=sup_mu h_mu (38.06.02 measure-theoretic / Kolmogorov-Sinai side) and to thermodynamic formalism (pressure of zero potential). Walters Ch. 7. Lean status none: Mathlib has compactness/finite subcovers, Subadditive/Fekete, the doubling map and shift as set-level objects, but no open-cover quantity N(U) or its subadditivity, no AKM h_top, no Bowen metric / (n,eps)-separated-spanning sets or their equivalence, no h(f^m)=m h(f) / product / factor laws, and none of the model evaluations (full shift, SFT Perron root, expanding-map degree, toral-automorphism eigenvalue sum) or the Yomdin-Newhouse volume-growth identity.

### dynamics.livsic-cohomological-rigidity-theorem

- **title**: The Livšic Cohomological Rigidity Theorem
- **unit**: `38.07.04`
- **prerequisites**: `38.03.01` (hyperbolic sets, Anosov/Axiom A, stable and unstable manifolds), `38.03.04` (shadowing, Anosov closing lemma, structural stability), `38.07.03` (the Hopf argument, ergodicity of Anosov systems)
- **notes**: stub - see unit `38.07.04`. The cohomological equation phi = u of-f minus u over a transitive Anosov (locally maximal hyperbolic) system; coboundary, transfer function u, cohomologous functions. Birkhoff cocycle S_n phi(x) and the periodic-orbit obstruction S_n phi(p) = sum over k from 0 to n-1 of phi(f^k p) for periodic p = f^n p. Livšic theorem (1971): for Hölder phi with vanishing periodic sums there is a Hölder u solving phi = u of-f minus u, unique up to an additive constant; proof reconstructs u along a dense orbit (u at f^n x_0 equals S_n phi(x_0)) and proves the Hölder estimate by the Anosov closing lemma (38.03.04) replacing a near-return by a true periodic orbit whose vanishing sum bounds the cocycle by K delta^alpha via the exponential closing/shadowing bound. Periodic-orbit obstruction is the COMPLETE invariant of Hölder cohomology (two Hölder functions cohomologous iff equal periodic sums). Measurable Livšic theorem (1972): a measurable a.e.-solution is a.e. equal to a Hölder one, proved by the Hopf stable/unstable-saturation argument (38.07.03) plus absolute continuity. Regularity (Livšic-de la Llave-Marco-Moriyón 1986): C-infinity system plus coboundary gives C-infinity transfer function via smoothness along stable/unstable foliations plus Journé gluing lemma; C^r gives C^{r minus eps}, analytic gives analytic. Applications: rigidity of equilibrium states/SRB measures (same Livšic class equals same equilibrium state, 38.06.04), periodic-data rigidity (matching periodic eigenvalues gives smooth conjugacy), marked-length-spectrum rigidity (Otal-Croke), vector/group-valued Livšic (Parry-Pollicott, Schmidt). Katok-Hasselblatt Ch. 19; Livšic 1971, 1972. Lean status none: Mathlib lacks the cohomological-equation/coboundary predicate, the periodic-orbit obstruction, the Livšic theorem and its closing-lemma proof, the measurable Livšic theorem, and the de la Llave-Marco-Moriyón regularity theory.


### operator-algebras.c-star-algebra-basics

- **title**: C*-Algebras: Axioms, Spectrum, and the Continuous Functional Calculus
- **unit**: `39.01.01`
- **prerequisites**: `02.11.04` (Banach space fundamentals), `02.11.01` (bounded linear operators), `02.11.08` (Hilbert space)
- **notes**: stub - see unit `39.01.01`. Banach algebras and C*-algebras with the C*-axiom $\|a^*a\|=\|a\|^2$; examples $B(H)$, $C_0(X)$, the compacts $K(H)$, $M_n(\mathbb{C})$. The spectrum $\sigma(a)$, spectral radius formula $r(a)=\lim\|a^n\|^{1/n}$, Gelfand-Mazur. Self-adjoint/normal/positive elements, the positivity order and $a^*a\ge 0$. The continuous functional calculus for a normal element: the isometric *-isomorphism $C(\sigma(a))\cong C^*(a,1)$ sending $f\mapsto f(a)$, proved via Gelfand theory of the commutative subalgebra (character space, Gelfand transform, spectral mapping). Approximate units; closed ideals and quotients are C*-algebras; commutative Gelfand-Naimark $C_0(X)\leftrightarrow X$. Davidson Ch. I; Murphy Ch. 1-2; Gelfand-Naimark 1943. Distinct from the finite-dim spectral theorem (01.01.13) and the Hilbert-space Borel calculus (02.11.03). Lean status none: Mathlib has CStarRing, spectrum, spectral-radius formula, and cfc/cfcHom but not the Gelfand-theory derivation packaged as one named theorem.


### operator-algebras.amenable-groups-folner-invariant-means

- **title**: Amenable Groups: Invariant Means, the Følner Condition, and Paradoxical Decompositions
- **unit**: `39.05.06`
- **prerequisites**: `01.02.20` (free group, free product, group presentation), `02.07.01` (sigma-algebra, measurable space, Borel sigma-algebra)
- **notes**: stub - see unit `39.05.06`. A discrete group G is amenable if it admits a left-invariant mean on l^infty(G). Equivalent characterisations (von Neumann-Day): the Følner condition (finite sets F_n with |gF_n △ F_n|/|F_n| → 0), Reiter's property (P_1) (approximately invariant l^1 probability vectors), the fixed-point property (every affine action on a compact convex set has a fixed point), Hulanicki's weak-containment criterion, Kesten's spectral-radius criterion (||lambda(mu)||=1), and C*_r(G)=C*(G). Closure: the amenable class is closed under subgroups, quotients, extensions, and directed unions (elementary amenable groups). Examples: finite, abelian, solvable, subexponential-growth groups; non-examples: F_2 is non-amenable via its explicit paradoxical decomposition (partition reduced words by first letter). Tarski's theorem: amenable iff not paradoxical; Banach-Tarski follows because SO(3) contains F_2. Von Neumann conjecture (non-amenable iff contains F_2) refuted by Ol'shanskii's Tarski monsters (1980) and Adian's Burnside groups. Operator-algebra payoff (Brown-Ozawa Ch. 2): C*_r(G) is nuclear iff G amenable, L(G) injective iff G amenable (Connes); Følner sets give the completely positive finite-rank approximations. Lean status none: Mathlib has invariant means and amenability of abelian/finite groups but lacks the Følner equivalence, Reiter/Kesten, the Day fixed-point theorem, the closure theorems, the paradoxical-decomposition/Tarski theory, and the F_2 non-amenability proof.


### operator-algebras.commutative-c-star-gelfand-duality

- **title**: Commutative C*-Algebras and Gelfand Duality
- **unit**: `39.01.02`
- **prerequisites**: `39.01.01` (C*-algebras: axioms, spectrum, continuous functional calculus), `02.01.05` (metric space), `02.11.04` (Banach space fundamentals)
- **notes**: stub - see unit `39.01.02`. The Gelfand transform $\Gamma(a)(\tau)=\tau(a)$ and the commutative Gelfand-Naimark theorem: every commutative unital C*-algebra $A$ is isometrically *-isomorphic to $C(\widehat A)$, where $\widehat A$ is the character space / maximal ideal space (nonzero homomorphisms $A\to\mathbb{C}$ with the weak-* topology, compact Hausdorff by Banach-Alaoglu); non-unital $A\cong C_0(\widehat A)$ with $\widehat A$ locally compact via unitisation / one-point compactification. Isometry from the C*-axiom on self-adjoints + spectral radius formula; surjectivity by Stone-Weierstrass; $\hat a(\widehat A)=\sigma(a)$; characters = points = maximal ideals. Gelfand duality as the contravariant equivalence $\mathsf{CommC^*}^{\mathrm{op}}\simeq\mathsf{CHaus}$ (spectrum functor, $X\mapsto C(X)$ quasi-inverse, evaluation unit and Gelfand counit) — the conceptual basis of noncommutative topology. The continuous functional calculus (39.01.01) is the single-generator case ($C^*(a,1)$, character space $\sigma(a)$). Davidson Ch. I; Murphy Ch. 2; Gelfand-Naimark 1943; Connes Ch. I. Lean status none: Mathlib has characterSpace, gelfandTransform, gelfandStarTransform but not the full duality equivalence (functor on morphisms, unit/counit, $C_0$ non-unital version) as one named theorem.


### operator-algebras.von-neumann-algebras-bicommutant

- **title**: Von Neumann Algebras and the Bicommutant Theorem
- **unit**: `39.03.01`
- **prerequisites**: `39.01.01` (C*-algebras: axioms, spectrum, continuous functional calculus), `02.11.08` (Hilbert space), `02.11.01` (bounded linear operators)
- **notes**: stub - see unit `39.03.01`. The operator topologies on $B(H)$ (norm, strong/SOT, weak/WOT, $\sigma$-weak/ultraweak/$\sigma$-strong) and their order of refinement; SOT=$\sigma$-strong and WOT=$\sigma$-weak on bounded sets; multiplication separately-but-not-jointly SOT-continuous, adjoint WOT- but not SOT-continuous. The commutant $M'$ of a self-adjoint set (always a WOT-closed unital $*$-subalgebra), the bicommutant $M''$, with $M\subseteq M''$, $M'=M'''$. Von Neumann's bicommutant theorem: for a unital $*$-subalgebra $M\subseteq B(H)$, $M''=M\iff M$ SOT-closed $\iff M$ WOT-closed; full proof via the single-vector case (projection $P$ onto $\overline{M\xi}$ lies in $M'$ by invariance of the cyclic subspace) and amplification to $H^n$ (commutant of $\pi(M)$ is $M_n(M')$). Von Neumann algebras as concrete W*-algebras; role of unitality/non-degeneracy. Centre $Z(M)=M\cap M'$; factors (trivial centre) as building blocks; direct-integral decomposition. Examples: $B(H)$ (a factor, $B(H)'=\mathbb{C}1$), $L^\infty(X,\mu)$ acting by multiplication (a MASA, $M'=M$), group von Neumann algebras $L(G)=\lambda(G)''$ with commutation theorem $L(G)'=R(G)$ and i.c.c.$\Rightarrow$ II$_1$ factor. Borel functional calculus and spectral projections inside $W^*(a)=\{a\}''$; Kaplansky density; Sakai predual characterisation; Murray-von Neumann type I/II/III classification. Takesaki Ch. II; Dixmier Ch. I; von Neumann 1930; Murray-von Neumann 1936-43. Lean status none: Mathlib lacks the `commutant` of a self-adjoint set, the `VonNeumannAlgebra` predicate ($M=M''$), the bicommutant theorem with its amplification/cyclic-projection proof, the Borel calculus landing in the generated algebra, the centre, and factors.

### operator-algebras.spectral-triples-reconstruction-theorem

- **title**: Spectral Triples and the Reconstruction Theorem
- **unit**: `39.06.01`
- **prerequisites**: `39.01.01` (C*-algebra basics, continuous functional calculus), `03.09.08` (Dirac operator on a spin manifold), `02.11.03` (unbounded self-adjoint operators)
- **notes**: stub - see unit `39.06.01`. A spectral triple $(\mathcal{A},\mathcal{H},D)$: a unital $*$-algebra $\mathcal{A}$ represented on a Hilbert space $\mathcal{H}$ with a self-adjoint Dirac operator $D$ such that $[D,a]$ is bounded for $a\in\mathcal{A}$ and $D$ has compact resolvent. Even/odd triples and the grading $\gamma$ ($\gamma D=-D\gamma$); real structure $J$ with $KO$-dimension $n\bmod 8$, the commutant (zeroth-order) and first-order conditions. The commutative example $(C^\infty(M),L^2(M,S),\not{\!D})$ recovers a compact spin manifold: $[\not{\!D},f]=c(df)$ bounded, ellipticity gives compact resolvent. Metric/spectral dimension via eigenvalue growth of $|D|$ ($\mu_n\sim c\,n^{1/p}$, $|D|^{-1}\in\mathcal{L}^{p,\infty}$); dimension spectrum as poles of $\zeta_b(s)=\operatorname{Tr}(b|D|^{-s})$. Connes' distance formula $d(\varphi,\psi)=\sup\{|\varphi(a)-\psi(a)|:\|[D,a]\|\le1\}$ with $\|[\not{\!D},f]\|=\operatorname{Lip}(f)$. The seven axioms: regularity, finiteness, reality/$J$, first order, orientation (Hochschild $n$-cycle whose representative is $\gamma$), Poincaré duality, dimension. Connes' reconstruction theorem (2013): a commutative regular oriented finite real spectral triple IS the canonical triple of a smooth compact spin manifold — manifolds = commutative spectral geometries, noncommutative algebras obeying the axioms are noncommutative manifolds. Dixmier trace / Connes trace theorem $\operatorname{Tr}_\omega(f|D|^{-n})=c_n\int_M f\,d\mathrm{vol}$; Weyl law gives metric dimension $=\dim M$. Connes Ch. VI; Connes 1996, 2013; Connes-Marcolli; Gracia-Bondía-Várilly-Figueroa Ch. 9-11. Lean status none: Mathlib lacks the bundled spectral-triple object, Schatten-Dixmier ideals, dimension spectrum, regularity algebra, Hochschild-orientation cocycle, and real-structure $J$ data.


### operator-algebras.states-gns-gelfand-naimark

- **title**: States, the GNS Construction, and the Gelfand-Naimark Representation Theorem
- **unit**: `39.01.03`
- **prerequisites**: `39.01.01` (C*-algebras: axioms, spectrum, continuous functional calculus), `39.01.02` (commutative C*-algebras and Gelfand duality), `02.11.08` (Hilbert space)
- **notes**: stub - see unit `39.01.03`. A state is a positive linear functional ($\varphi(a^*a)\ge0$) of norm 1 (unital: $\varphi(1)=1$); the state space $S(A)$ is a weak-* compact convex set (Banach-Alaoglu), pure states are its extreme points (Krein-Milman). The GNS construction: from a state $\varphi$ build the sesquilinear form $\langle a,b\rangle_\varphi=\varphi(b^*a)$, the null left ideal $N_\varphi=\{a:\varphi(a^*a)=0\}$, the completion $H_\varphi$ of $A/N_\varphi$, the left-regular representation $\pi_\varphi(a)[b]=[ab]$ and cyclic vector $\xi_\varphi=[1]$ with $\varphi(a)=\langle\pi_\varphi(a)\xi_\varphi,\xi_\varphi\rangle$, unique up to unitary equivalence. The universal representation $\pi_u=\bigoplus_{\varphi\in S(A)}\pi_\varphi$ is isometric (states attaining $\varphi(a^*a)=\|a\|^2$ via functional calculus + Hahn-Banach), giving the noncommutative Gelfand-Naimark theorem: every C*-algebra is isometrically *-isomorphic to a norm-closed *-subalgebra of $B(H)$ — the abstract characterisation of C*-algebras. Pure states $\iff$ irreducible representations (dominated-functional/commutant order isomorphism + Schur's lemma); the noncommutative spectrum $\widehat A$ = pure states modulo unitary equivalence, collapsing to the character space in the commutative case. Existence of states via functional calculus + Hahn-Banach; order recovered as $a\ge0\iff\varphi(a)\ge0\ \forall\varphi$. Davidson Ch. I; Murphy Ch. 3; Gelfand-Naimark 1943; Segal 1947. Lean status none: Mathlib lacks the state space as a named weak-* compact convex set and the GNS construction (form, null ideal, completion, left-regular representation, cyclic vector) and the noncommutative Gelfand-Naimark embedding as consolidated theorems.

### operator-algebras.predual-normal-states-sigma-weak

- **title**: The Predual, Normal States, and the σ-Weak Topology
- **unit**: `39.03.02`
- **prerequisites**: `39.03.01` (von Neumann algebras, bicommutant theorem), `02.11.08` (Hilbert space), `02.11.04` (Banach space fundamentals)
- **notes**: stub - see unit `39.03.02`. Normal positive functionals/states on a von Neumann algebra $M$: order-continuous on bounded increasing nets $\iff$ completely additive on orthogonal projections $\iff$ $\sigma$-weakly continuous $\iff$ of the form $a\mapsto\mathrm{Tr}(\rho a)$ for trace-class $\rho$. The predual $M_*$ (the $\sigma$-weakly continuous = normal functionals) as the unique Banach space with $(M_*)^*\cong M$ isometrically; the $\sigma$-weak (ultraweak) topology as $\sigma(M,M_*)$ and the $\sigma$-strong topology from $a\mapsto\varphi(a^*a)^{1/2}$. Sakai's theorem: a C*-algebra is a W*-/von Neumann algebra iff it is isometric to a dual Banach space, predual unique — the abstract characterisation parallel to Gelfand-Naimark. Normal vs singular functionals and the Takesaki (noncommutative Lebesgue) decomposition $\varphi=\varphi_n+\varphi_s$. $\sigma$-weak continuity of multiplication on bounded sets; $\sigma$-weak = WOT and $\sigma$-strong = SOT on the unit ball; adjoint $\sigma$-weakly continuous. Normal $*$-homomorphisms ($\sigma$-weakly continuous; kernel $=Mz$ for a central projection); the functor $M\mapsto M_*$ as the noncommutative-measure-theory mirror of Gelfand duality. Commutative model $L^\infty(X,\mu)=(L^1)^*$, normal states = probability densities, singular = finitely-additive part. C*-algebras = noncommutative topology, von Neumann algebras = noncommutative measure theory. Takesaki Ch. II-III; Sakai 1956; Dixmier Ch. I. Lean status none: Mathlib lacks the `predual` of a von Neumann algebra, the `normalState` predicate, Sakai's dual-space characterisation with predual uniqueness, the normality equivalence theorem, and the Takesaki decomposition.


### operator-algebras.kaplansky-density-theorem

- **title**: The Kaplansky Density Theorem
- **unit**: `39.03.03`
- **prerequisites**: `39.03.01` (von Neumann algebras and the bicommutant theorem), `39.01.01` (C*-algebras: axioms, spectrum, continuous functional calculus)
- **notes**: stub - see unit `39.03.03`. For a $*$-subalgebra $A\subseteq B(H)$ with strong closure $M=\overline A^{\,\mathrm{SOT}}=A''$, the unit ball of $A$ is SOT-dense in the unit ball of $M$, with the self-adjoint, positive, and unitary refinements densifying their counterparts under norm control. Proof: reduce to a C*-algebra; self-adjoint case via the SOT-continuous Cayley-type reshaping $f(t)=2t/(1+t^2)$ (bounded by 1, $\|f(a)\|\le1$ by spectral mapping, SOT-continuity from the resolvent identity $f(a)-f(c)=2g(a)[(a-c)+a(c-a)c]g(c)$ with $g=(1+a^2)^{-1}$); general case via the $2\times2$ off-diagonal self-adjoint dilation $\tilde x=\begin{pmatrix}0&x\\x^*&0\end{pmatrix}$ reading off the $(1,2)$ entry; unitaries via $u=e^{ib}$ and SOT-continuity of $a\mapsto e^{ia}$. Joint SOT-continuity of multiplication holds on bounded sets only. Applications: extending normal/completely-positive maps from a strongly dense C*-subalgebra to its von Neumann completion, GNS and standard-form constructions, comparison of projections. Contrast: the naive cut-off $\chi=\max(-1,\min(1,\cdot))$ is not SOT-continuous on self-adjoints. Takesaki Ch. II §4; Kadison-Ringrose §5.3 Thm 5.3.5; Kaplansky 1951. Lean status none: Mathlib lacks the commutant/von Neumann layer, the density theorem with its self-adjoint/positive/unitary refinements, and the SOT-continuity of the reshaping transform on top of `cfc`.

### operator-algebras.dixmier-trace-noncommutative-integral

- **title**: The Dixmier Trace and the Noncommutative Integral
- **unit**: `39.06.05`
- **prerequisites**: `39.06.01` (spectral triples and the reconstruction theorem), `02.11.05` (compact operators: singular values, Schatten ideals)
- **notes**: stub - see unit `39.06.05`. The Dixmier ideal $\mathcal{L}^{1,\infty}$ (Macaev / weak trace-class operators, $\mu_n(T)=O(1/n)$, partial sums $\sigma_N=\sum_{n<N}\mu_n=O(\log N)$) and the Dixmier trace $\operatorname{Tr}_\omega(T)=\omega(\frac{1}{\log N}\sum_{n<N}\mu_n(T))$ built from a dilation-invariant state $\omega$ on divergent log-averages: a singular (non-normal) trace, unitarily invariant, $\operatorname{Tr}_\omega(ST)=\operatorname{Tr}_\omega(TS)$, vanishing on trace-class operators. Measurability = $\omega$-independence = convergence of $\sigma_N/\log N$. Connes' trace theorem: for a classical $\Psi$DO of order $-n$ on an $n$-manifold, $\operatorname{Tr}_\omega(P)=\frac{1}{n(2\pi)^n}\operatorname{Res}_W(P)$ = symbol integral over the cosphere bundle = the Wodzicki residue (the unique trace on $\Psi$DOs). So the Dixmier trace IS the noncommutative integral $\fint T:=\operatorname{Tr}_\omega(T)$; in a spectral triple $\fint a:=\operatorname{Tr}_\omega(a|D|^{-n})$ recovers $\int_M a\,\sqrt g$ up to $c_n$. Heat-kernel and zeta-residue formulas ($\operatorname{Tr}_\omega(T)=\operatorname{Res}_{s=1}\operatorname{Tr}(T^s)$, Tauberian); role in the spectral action and preview of the local index formula (39.06.06). Dixmier 1966; Connes 1988; Connes Ch. IV; Connes-Marcolli; Gracia-Bondia-Varilly-Figueroa Ch. 7; Lord-Sukochev-Zanin. Lean status none: Mathlib lacks singular-value rearrangement, the Macaev ideal, the dilation-invariant state, the Dixmier trace, measurability, and the Wodzicki residue.


### operator-algebras.connes-distance-formula

- **title**: The Connes Distance Formula
- **unit**: `39.06.02`
- **prerequisites**: `39.06.01` (spectral triples and the reconstruction theorem), `39.01.02` (commutative C*-algebras and Gelfand duality)
- **notes**: stub - see unit `39.06.02`. The Connes distance formula $d_D(\varphi,\psi)=\sup\{|\varphi(a)-\psi(a)|:a\in\mathcal{A},\,\|[D,a]\|\le1\}$ defines a (possibly $+\infty$) extended metric on the state space $\mathcal{S}(A)$ of a spectral triple $(\mathcal{A},\mathcal{H},D)$, via the Lipschitz seminorm $L(a)=\|[D,a]\|$ with kernel the scalars. Self-adjoint and constant-shift reductions of the supremum. Commutative reduction: for the canonical triple $(C^\infty(M),L^2(M,S),\not{\!D})$ of a closed spin manifold, $[\not{\!D},f]=c(df)$ and $\|c(df)\|=\||df|_g\|_\infty=\operatorname{Lip}_g(f)$, so on pure states (Dirac masses $\delta_p$, the points via Gelfand duality) the formula returns the geodesic distance $d_g(p,q)$ — "the metric is encoded in $D$". Genuine extended metric (symmetry, triangle inequality before passing to sup, separation under irreducibility $\{a:[D,a]=0\}=\mathbb{C}1$). Finite/matrix examples: the two-point space $\mathbb{C}\oplus\mathbb{C}$ with off-diagonal $D$ gives $\|[D,a]\|=|m||a_1-a_2|$ and $d(\delta_1,\delta_2)=1/|m|$; mixed states make the state space a segment of length $1/|m|$ — the basis of the NCG Standard Model internal Higgs distance ($\mathcal{A}_F=\mathbb{C}\oplus\mathbb{H}\oplus M_3(\mathbb{C})$, Yukawa/inverse-mass lengths). Monge-Kantorovich/optimal-transport duality: on $C(X)$ states are probability measures and $d_D=W_1$ (Kantorovich-Rubinstein dual of Wasserstein-1), the noncommutative generalisation of optimal transport. Rieffel Lip-norms / compact quantum metric spaces guarantee $d_D$ metrizes the weak-* topology; product (almost-commutative) distances are not simply Pythagorean. Connes 1989, Connes Ch. VI §1 (1994); Rieffel 1999; Iochum-Krajewski-Martinetti 2001. Lean status none: Mathlib lacks the Lipschitz-seminorm $\to$ state-space extended-metric construction, Rieffel's CQMS axioms, and $\|[\not{\!D},f]\|=\operatorname{Lip}_g(f)$.


### operator-algebras.cyclic-separating-vectors-standard-form

- **title**: Cyclic and Separating Vectors and the Standard Form
- **unit**: `39.04.01`
- **prerequisites**: `39.03.01` (von Neumann algebras and the bicommutant theorem), `39.01.03` (states, the GNS construction, the Gelfand-Naimark theorem)
- **notes**: stub - see unit `39.04.01`. A vector $\xi$ is cyclic for a von Neumann algebra $M\subseteq B(H)$ when $\overline{M\xi}=H$ and separating when $a\xi=0,\,a\in M\Rightarrow a=0$; the duality lemma: $\xi$ cyclic for $M$ iff separating for $M'$ (projection onto $\overline{M\xi}$ lies in $M'$), so a cyclic-separating $\xi$ is cyclic-separating for both $M$ and $M'$. Existence: the GNS representation of a faithful normal state $\omega$ is normal and faithful with $\xi_\omega$ cyclic-separating ($\omega$ faithful $\Leftrightarrow$ $\xi_\omega$ separating); a faithful normal state exists when $M_*$ is separable ($\sum 2^{-n}\omega_n$ with supports $\nearrow 1$). The standard form $(M,H,J,\mathfrak{P})$: antiunitary modular conjugation $J=J^*=J^{-1}$ with $JMJ=M'$ and $JcJ=c^*$ on $Z(M)$, self-dual cone $\mathfrak{P}=\overline{\{aJaJ\xi\}}$ with $J|_\mathfrak{P}=\mathrm{id}$ and $aJaJ\mathfrak{P}\subseteq\mathfrak{P}$. Haagerup 1975: existence for every von Neumann algebra and uniqueness up to a unique cone-preserving unitary; every normal state is a unique vector state $\omega_\xi$, $\xi\in\mathfrak{P}$ (Powers-Stormer metric); every normal automorphism is spatially implemented by a unique $\mathfrak{P}$-preserving unitary commuting with $J$. Seeds the modular operator: $S_0:a\xi\mapsto a^*\xi$ is well defined (separating) and densely defined (cyclic), closable, with closure $S=J\Delta^{1/2}$ for Tomita's theorem (39.04.02). Takesaki Ch. VI; Haagerup Math. Scand. 37 (1975); Bratteli-Robinson §2.5; Reeh-Schlieder 1961 (cyclic-separating QFT vacuum). Lean status none: Mathlib lacks the von Neumann-algebra predicate, the cyclic/separating predicates and their duality, and the standard form with its self-dual cone and $JMJ=M'$.


### operator-algebras.toeplitz-cuntz-algebras-extensions

- **title**: The Toeplitz Algebra, Cuntz Algebras, and Extensions
- **unit**: `39.01.04`
- **prerequisites**: `39.01.03` (states, the GNS construction, the Gelfand-Naimark representation theorem), `39.01.01` (C*-algebras: axioms, spectrum, continuous functional calculus), `02.11.05` (compact operators)
- **notes**: stub - see unit `39.01.04`. The Toeplitz algebra $\mathcal{T}=C^*(S)$ of the unilateral shift $S$ ($S^*S=1$, $SS^*=1-p_0$); the Toeplitz extension $0\to K\to\mathcal{T}\xrightarrow{\sigma}C(\mathbb{T})\to0$ with symbol map $\sigma(S)=z$, the compacts as kernel, Toeplitz operators $T_f=PM_fP$ on $H^2$, Fredholmness for nowhere-zero symbol and $\operatorname{index}(T_f)=-\operatorname{wind}(f)$ (Coburn; the index map as $\partial:K_1(C(\mathbb{T}))\to K_0(K)=\mathbb{Z}$). Universal C*-algebras by generators and relations (supremum-of-representations norm, admissible relations). Cuntz algebras $O_n=C^*(s_1,\dots,s_n\mid s_i^*s_j=\delta_{ij}1,\ \sum s_is_i^*=1)$: simple, separable, nuclear, purely infinite, $K_0=\mathbb{Z}/(n-1)$, $K_1=0$ (gauge action, UHF core $M_{n^\infty}$); $O_\infty$ ($K_0=\mathbb{Z}$); Cuntz-Krieger $O_A$ of a $0$-$1$ matrix with $K_0=\operatorname{coker}(1-A^t)$ (subshifts of finite type / Bowen-Franks). Extensions of C*-algebras and the Busby invariant $\tau:B\to Q(J)=M(J)/J$ (pullback reconstruction); the Calkin algebra $Q(H)=B(H)/K$; BDF theory $\mathrm{Ext}(X)\cong K^1(X)$, essentially normal operators classified by essential spectrum + index data, $\mathrm{Ext}(\mathbb{T})\cong\mathbb{Z}$. Davidson Ch. V, IX; Cuntz 1977; Coburn 1967; Busby 1968; Brown-Douglas-Fillmore 1977. Lean status none: Mathlib lacks the Toeplitz algebra/extension, universal C*-algebras by generators and relations, the Cuntz/Cuntz-Krieger algebras with simplicity and pure infiniteness, and the Calkin algebra with the Busby invariant and Ext group.


### operator-algebras.af-algebras-bratteli-irrational-rotation

- **title**: AF Algebras, Bratteli Diagrams, and the Irrational Rotation Algebra
- **unit**: `39.02.01`
- **prerequisites**: `39.01.03` (states, GNS, Gelfand-Naimark), `39.01.01` (C*-algebras: axioms, spectrum, continuous functional calculus)
- **notes**: stub - see unit `39.02.01`. Approximately finite-dimensional (AF) C*-algebras as inductive limits of finite-dimensional C*-algebras (direct sums of matrix blocks); Bratteli diagrams encoding the multiplicity matrices of the connecting embeddings; Elliott's theorem (1976) classifying unital AF algebras by the scaled ordered K_0 group / dimension group (K_0(A), K_0(A)_+, scale) via V(A) = Murray-von Neumann classes and Grothendieck completion, proved by finite-stage multiplicity-matrix lifting + approximate intertwining - the first instance of the Elliott program. Examples: UHF algebras classified by supernatural numbers, the CAR/Fermion algebra M_{2^inf} with K_0 = Z[1/2], commutative AF = C(X) for X totally disconnected (Cantor set, dimension group C(X,Z), Cantor minimal systems). The irrational rotation algebra A_theta = C*(u,v : vu = e^{2 pi i theta} uv) (noncommutative torus = crossed product C(T) x_theta Z), simple with unique trace tau(u^m v^n) = delta_{(m,n),0}; Pimsner-Voiculescu six-term sequence gives K_0 = K_1 = Z^2, Rieffel projection has tau(e_theta) = theta so tau_*(K_0) = Z + theta Z, and A_theta = A_theta' iff theta' = +/- theta mod 1 - a landmark of noncommutative topology (Davidson Ch. III, VI; Effros; Elliott 1976; Bratteli 1972; Glimm 1960; Rieffel 1981). Lean status none: Mathlib has no C*-inductive limit, no AF class, no K_0 functor / ordered group / dimension group, no Elliott theorem, and none of the rotation-algebra K-theory.

### operator-algebras.comparison-projections-type-classification

- **title**: Comparison of Projections and the Murray-von Neumann Type Classification
- **unit**: `39.03.04`
- **prerequisites**: `39.03.01` (von Neumann algebras and the bicommutant theorem), `39.03.02` (the predual, normal states, and the σ-weak topology)
- **notes**: stub - see unit `39.03.04`. Murray–von Neumann equivalence $p\sim q$ via a partial isometry $v\in M$ with $v^*v=p$, $vv^*=q$; the order $p\preceq q$ and the comparison theorem (a central projection $z$ with $zp\preceq zq$ and $(1-z)q\preceq(1-z)p$; total comparability in a factor). Finite/infinite/properly-infinite/abelian/purely-infinite projections; central support $c(p)$. Type decomposition $1=z_I+z_{II}+z_{III}$ (and $I_n$ via $B(K)\bar\otimes A$, $II_1$/$II_\infty$), factors of each type ($M_n$, $B(H)$, $L(G)$ i.c.c., $R\bar\otimes B(\ell^2)$, Powers $R_\lambda$). Unique faithful normal trace and dimension function on a finite factor: range $\{0,1/n,\dots,1\}$ for $I_n$, the full interval $[0,1]$ (continuous dimension) for $II_1$; $p\preceq q\iff\tau(p)\le\tau(q)$. Hyperfinite $II_1$ factor $R=\overline{\bigcup_n M_{2^n}(\mathbb C)}$ and Murray–von Neumann uniqueness; fundamental group $\mathcal F(M)$; existence of type III (Powers, Connes III$_\lambda$). Takesaki Ch. V; Murray–von Neumann 1936/1943; Dixmier Ch. III. Lean status none: Mathlib lacks the equivalence relation, the comparison theorem, the finite/infinite/abelian distinctions, the type decomposition, the trace/dimension function, and the hyperfinite-factor construction.

### operator-algebras.completely-positive-maps-stinespring-dilation

- **title**: Completely Positive Maps and the Stinespring Dilation Theorem
- **unit**: `39.05.01`
- **prerequisites**: `39.01.03` (states, the GNS construction, the Gelfand-Naimark representation theorem), `39.01.01` (C*-algebras: axioms, spectrum, continuous functional calculus)
- **notes**: stub - see unit `39.05.01`. Positive vs. completely positive (CP) maps $\varphi:A\to B$ between C*-algebras: $\varphi$ is $n$-positive if the amplification $\varphi_n=\varphi\otimes\mathrm{id}_n$ is positive on $M_n(A)$, completely positive if $n$-positive for all $n$. Canonical positive-but-not-CP map: the transpose on $M_2$ (Choi matrix = swap, eigenvalue $-1$); the Peres-Horodecki entanglement-criterion core. **Stinespring dilation** (Stinespring 1955): every CP $\varphi:A\to B(H)$ has $\varphi(a)=V^*\pi(a)V$ for a unital $*$-representation $\pi$ on a larger $K$ and bounded $V$ with $\|V\|^2=\|\varphi(1)\|$; proof is GNS amplified to matrices on $A\odot H$ with form $\langle a\otimes\xi,b\otimes\eta\rangle=\langle\varphi(b^*a)\xi,\eta\rangle$ (complete positivity = positive semidefiniteness), quotient by null space, complete, left-multiplication representation, $V\xi=[1\otimes\xi]$. Minimal dilation $\overline{\pi(A)VH}=K$ unique up to unitary equivalence; states are the scalar case $H=\mathbb{C}$. **Choi's theorem** (Choi 1975): for $\varphi:M_n\to M_m$, CP $\iff$ $n$-positive $\iff$ Choi matrix $C_\varphi=[\varphi(e_{ij})]\ge0$ $\iff$ Kraus/operator-sum $\varphi(a)=\sum_k V_k^*aV_k$ ($\le nm$ terms); Choi rank = rank $C_\varphi$ = minimal Kraus count; Choi-Jamiolkowski correspondence. **Kadison-Schwarz inequality** $\varphi(a)^*\varphi(a)\le\varphi(a^*a)$ for unital 2-positive $\varphi$ (via $\varphi_2$ on $\begin{pmatrix}a^*a&a^*\\a&1\end{pmatrix}$ + Schur complement), with Stinespring defect $V^*\pi(a)^*(1-VV^*)\pi(a)V$. **Multiplicative domain** $A_\varphi=\{a:\varphi(a^*a)=\varphi(a)^*\varphi(a),\varphi(aa^*)=\varphi(a)\varphi(a)^*\}$: the largest C*-subalgebra where $\varphi$ is a $*$-homomorphism, $=\{a:\pi(a)V=V\varphi(a)\}$, with bimodule property $\varphi(bab')=\varphi(b)\varphi(a)\varphi(b')$ for $b,b'\in A_\varphi$; conditional expectations as the model. Russo-Dye ($\|\varphi\|=\|\varphi(1)\|$, UCP = unital contractions), matrix state spaces $S_n(A)$. **Arveson's extension theorem** (Arveson 1969): CP on an operator system $S\subseteq A$ extends CP to $A$ (Hahn-Banach for ordered operator spaces; injectivity of $B(H)$); preview of 39.05.02 and the CP-approximation definition of nuclearity/exactness. Cross-ref finite-dim quantum-channel version 12.17.01 (distinct: C*-structure theory vs. quantum-information). Stinespring 1955, Kraus 1971, Choi 1975, Arveson 1969; Paulsen Ch. 3-6; Brown-Ozawa Ch. 1. Lean status none: Mathlib lacks the matrix-amplification / $M_n(A)$-order layer, CP maps as a class, the GNS construction, Stinespring, Choi, Kraus, Kadison-Schwarz, multiplicative domain, and Arveson extension.


### operator-algebras.operator-k-theory-k0-k1

- **title**: Operator K-Theory: K_0 and K_1 of C*-Algebras
- **unit**: `39.02.02`
- **prerequisites**: `39.02.01` (AF algebras, Bratteli diagrams, dimension groups), `39.01.01` (C*-algebras: axioms, spectrum, continuous functional calculus)
- **notes**: stub - see unit `39.02.02`. K_0(A) as the Grothendieck group of Murray-von Neumann equivalence classes of projections in M_infinity(A) = colim M_n(A) (standard picture; unitisation-corrected K_0 = ker(K_0(A~) -> K_0(C)) for non-unital A; positive cone K_0(A)_+ and order unit [1] for stably finite A). K_1(A) = pi_0(U_infinity(A)) the connected components of unitaries in the matrix stabilisation. The four pillars: functoriality, stability K_*(A (x) K) = K_*(A), homotopy invariance, half/split exactness and continuity (commutes with inductive limits). Six-term exact sequence with index map d:K_1(A/J)->K_0(J) and exponential map; Bott periodicity K_0(SA)=K_1(A). Standard computations: K_0(C)=Z, K_1(C)=0; K_0(M_n)=Z (matrix stability); K_0(B(H))=0, K_1(B(H))=0 (Eilenberg swindle / proper infiniteness / Kuiper); K_0(K)=Z; K_0 of an AF algebra = its dimension group (39.02.01); K_0(C(X))=K^0(X), K_1(C(X))=K^1(X) topological via Serre-Swan (projections over C(X) <-> vector bundles, contrast 03.08.01). Index map of the Toeplitz extension = Fredholm index = -winding; K_0(O_n)=Z/(n-1) (39.01.04). Operator K-theory extends topological K-theory to the noncommutative world; launch of the Elliott program and Kasparov KK-theory. Davidson Ch. IV-VII; Blackadar; Rordam-Larsen-Laustsen; Wegge-Olsen; Swan 1962; Atiyah-Hirzebruch. Lean status none: Mathlib lacks V(A), K_0/K_1 functors, the order/positive cone, stability, homotopy invariance, half-exactness, continuity, the index map, and the six-term sequence.

### operator-algebras.operator-systems-arveson-choi-effros

- **title**: Operator Systems, Arveson's Extension Theorem, and the Choi-Effros Theorem
- **unit**: `39.05.02`
- **prerequisites**: `39.05.01` (completely positive maps and the Stinespring dilation theorem), `39.01.01` (C*-algebras: axioms, spectrum, continuous functional calculus)
- **notes**: stub - see unit `39.05.02`. Operator systems: self-adjoint unital closed subspaces $S\subseteq A$ of a C*-algebra with the inherited matrix order $M_n(S)_+=M_n(S)\cap M_n(A)_+$ and order unit $1$; abstract operator systems = matrix-ordered $*$-vector spaces with an Archimedean matrix order unit; (unital) completely positive maps and complete order isomorphisms; operator spaces and Ruan's matrix-norm axioms. **Arveson's extension theorem** (Arveson 1969): a CP map $S\to B(H)$ extends to a CP map $A\to B(H)$ - Hahn-Banach for CP maps, proved by reducing to finite-dim $H=\mathbb C^m$, pairing CP maps with positive functionals on $M_m(S)$, order-unit Hahn-Banach extension to $M_m(A)$, pairing back; equivalently the injectivity of $B(H)$ in the operator-system category. **Choi-Effros theorem** (Choi-Effros 1977): an abstract matrix-ordered $*$-vector space with an Archimedean order unit is unitally completely order isomorphic to a concrete operator system (via separating matrix states $\bigoplus_\alpha\varphi_\alpha$); the matrix order, not the level-1 order, is the complete invariant (transpose on $M_2$ counterexample). **Choi-Effros product** $a\circ b=\Phi(ab)$ from a UCP idempotent $\Phi:B(H)\to B(H)$ makes an injective operator system $S=\Phi(B(H))$ a C*-algebra (fixed set in the multiplicative domain; Kadison-Schwarz equality). **Wittstock's** completely bounded extension and the **Paulsen $2\times2$ trick** reduce cb-maps to CP maps via the Paulsen system; Ruan's theorem = operator-space mirror of Choi-Effros. Injective = ranges of normal conditional expectations; injective/semidiscrete/hyperfinite/amenable for factors (Connes). Preview of nuclearity/exactness 39.05.03. Arveson 1969, Choi-Effros 1977, Ruan 1988, Wittstock 1981; Paulsen Ch. 6,13-15; Effros-Ruan; Brown-Ozawa Ch. 1. Lean status none: Mathlib lacks operator systems as a class, the matrix-ordered $*$-vector space / Archimedean order unit, CP maps between abstract ordered spaces, Arveson extension, Choi-Effros realisation and product, Ruan axioms, and Wittstock extension.

### operator-algebras.c-star-tensor-products-min-max-norms

- **title**: Tensor Products of C*-Algebras: the Minimal and Maximal Norms
- **unit**: `39.05.03`
- **prerequisites**: `39.05.01` (completely positive maps and the Stinespring dilation theorem), `39.01.03` (states, the GNS construction, the Gelfand-Naimark representation theorem)
- **notes**: stub - see unit `39.05.03`. The algebraic tensor product $A\odot B$ as a $*$-algebra (finite sums $\sum a_i\otimes b_i$) and the C*-norms it can carry. **Minimal (spatial) norm** $\|\cdot\|_{\min}$: from faithful representations $A\subseteq B(H)$, $B\subseteq B(K)$ acting on $H\otimes K$, independent of the representations (Takesaki). **Maximal norm** $\|\cdot\|_{\max}=\sup_\pi\|\pi(x)\|$ over all $*$-representations / pairs of commuting representations; universal/enveloping C*-algebra of $A\odot B$. Sandwich $\|\cdot\|_{\min}\le\gamma\le\|\cdot\|_{\max}$ for every C*-norm $\gamma$ ($\min$ least via norming product vector states + CP slice maps; $\max$ greatest via Gelfand-Naimark universality). **min $\ne$ max**: first example $C^*(F_2)\otimes C^*(F_2)$ (Takesaki 1964), certifying $C^*(F_2)$ non-nuclear (= $F_2$ non-amenable). $\min$ injective (subalgebra-stable), $\max$ projective (quotient-stable); slice maps $L_\varphi(a\otimes b)=\varphi(a)b$. Commuting-CP-map computation of $\max$; **Connes-Kirchberg**: $C^*(F_\infty)\otimes_{\min}C^*(F_\infty)=C^*(F_\infty)\otimes_{\max}C^*(F_\infty)\iff$ Connes embedding, refuted via $\mathrm{MIP}^*=\mathrm{RE}$ (2020). **Nuclearity** = $\min=\max$ for all $B$ (= CPAP, preview of 39.05.04); finite-dim, commutative, type I, CAR nuclear; free-group algebras not. Cross-ref finite-dim composite quantum systems 12.17.01 (matrix algebras nuclear). Takesaki 1964/1979, Lance 1973, Brown-Ozawa Ch. 3, Pisier 2020. Lean status none: Mathlib lacks any C*-norm on $A\odot B$ (min spatial norm + representation-independence, max universal norm, the sandwich, the completions).


### operator-algebras.tomita-theorem-modular-operator-conjugation

- **title**: Tomita's Theorem: the Modular Operator and Modular Conjugation
- **unit**: `39.04.02`
- **prerequisites**: `39.04.01` (cyclic and separating vectors and the standard form), `02.11.03` (unbounded self-adjoint operators)
- **notes**: stub - see unit `39.04.02`. For a von Neumann algebra $M\subseteq B(H)$ with cyclic-separating $\xi$, the closable antilinear Tomita operator $S_0:a\xi\mapsto a^*\xi$ ($a\in M$) has closure $S$ with polar decomposition $S=J\Delta^{1/2}$, defining the modular operator $\Delta=S^*S$ (positive self-adjoint, generally unbounded) and the modular conjugation $J$ (antiunitary involution, $J=J^*=J^{-1}$, $J^2=1$). Basic relations $J\Delta J=\Delta^{-1}$, $J\Delta^{it}=\Delta^{it}J$, $S^*=F=J\Delta^{-1/2}$, $\Delta\xi=\xi$, $J\xi=\xi$. **Tomita's theorem** (Tomita 1967, Takesaki 1970): $JMJ=M'$ (the conjugation spatially implements the commutant duality $M=M''$) and $\Delta^{it}M\Delta^{-it}=M$ for all $t$, so $\sigma_t=\mathrm{Ad}(\Delta^{it})$ is the modular automorphism group of $M$. Proof via strip-analyticity of $z\mapsto\Delta^{iz}a\Delta^{-iz}$ on $-\tfrac12\le\mathrm{Im}\,z\le0$, the symmetry $S^*=F=J\Delta^{-1/2}$ giving $JMJ\subseteq M'$ and its mirror $JM'J\subseteq M$, and $J\Delta^{it}=\Delta^{it}J$. **KMS condition**: $\sigma^\omega$ is the unique flow for which $\omega=\omega_\xi$ is KMS at $\beta=1$ (analytic on $0\le\mathrm{Im}\,z\le1$ with boundary swap $\omega(\sigma_t(a)b)\leftrightarrow\omega(b\sigma_t(a))$; Haag-Hugenholtz-Winnink 1967). First computations: trace case $\Delta=1$, $\sigma_t=\mathrm{id}$ (modular triviality $\Leftrightarrow$ traciality); finite-dim Gibbs case $\omega(a)=\operatorname{tr}(\rho a)$, $\xi=\rho^{1/2}$ gives $\Delta=L_\rho R_{\rho^{-1}}=\rho\otimes\rho^{-1}$, $Jx=x^*$, $\sigma_t(a)=\rho^{it}a\rho^{-it}$. Connes cocycle $(D\psi:D\omega)_t$ makes $[\sigma^\omega]\in\mathrm{Out}(M)$ state-independent; Connes spectrum $S(M)$ and $T(M)$ split type III$_\lambda$; Takesaki crossed-product duality realises type III as $N\rtimes_\theta\mathbb{R}$ of a tracial $N$; Bisognano-Wichmann identifies $\Delta^{it}$ of a wedge algebra with a Lorentz boost (Unruh effect). Takesaki Ch. VI-VIII; Bratteli-Robinson §2.5; Stratila Modular Theory; Connes 1973. Lean status none: Mathlib lacks the von Neumann-algebra predicate, the closable antilinear $S_0$, the polar decomposition of a conjugate-linear operator into antiunitary $J$ and positive $\Delta^{1/2}$, and Tomita's theorem $JMJ=M'$, $\Delta^{it}M\Delta^{-it}=M$.

### operator-algebras.noncommutative-torus-geometry
The noncommutative torus as a noncommutative geometry: smooth subalgebra $A_\theta^\infty$, derivations $\delta_1,\delta_2$, trace $\tau$, flat spectral triple, Gauss-Bonnet and modular scalar curvature, Heisenberg modules and $SL_2(\mathbb{Z})$ Morita equivalence, Connes-Rieffel Yang-Mills.

### operator-algebras.traces-continuous-dimension-ii1-factor

- **title**: Traces, Continuous Dimension, and the II_1 Factor
- **unit**: `39.03.05`
- **prerequisites**: `39.03.04` (comparison of projections and the Murray-von Neumann type classification), `39.03.02` (the predual, normal states, and the σ-weak topology)
- **notes**: stub - see unit `39.03.05`.
